programme.title: NCIS

Abby detects malware in the platform's computer systems, while Ducky finds traces on the victim's body that lead to a lawyer who works for the oil company. Back in the office, a prankster is bringing some light relief and although the likeliest suspect is Tony, he denies any involvement. Crime drama, guest starring Diane Neal

programme.desc: Gibbs is forced to take a step back from work when his increasingly frail father Jackson begs him to help track down an old friend from the Second World War who does not have long left to live. Crime drama, guest starring Ralph Waite (The Waltons), with Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Petty officer Jody Ray is killed by a hit-and-run driver and evidence points to staff sergeant Justin Dunne, whose guilt seems certain when DiNozzo and McGee find him washing a bloodstained knife when they go to arrest him. However, Dunne tells his lawyer Carrie Clark he was miles away in Falls Church at the time - stabbing bookmaker Donny Sullivan to death. Unable to reveal the truth to the NCIS team, Clark decides to check out the alibi herself. Crime drama, guest starring Salli Richardson-Whitfield (A Town Called Eureka)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An internal security breach forces NCIS to go into lockdown when a covert listening device is found in a pen belonging to Secretary of the Navy Sarah Porter. programme.desc: Too drunk to remember the details of the beating she took at a hospital, a teen sues a fellow partygoer for damages.

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: The vicious and repeated mauling of a Jack Russell Terrier by a neighbour's larger dog is disputed.

programme.title: Psychic Private Eyes
programme.desc: Cases of mysterious deaths and disappearances are re-opened as psychics Colin Fry, Tony Stockwell and TJ Higgs attempt to contact the dead and investigate unsolved crimes

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: The series skips seven years into the future after Joe's plane crashes over the Pacific and he is presumed dead. Now a lawyer, Allison has a vision in which he is still alive during a case

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Paranormal drama inspired by the life of medium Allison DuBois. Patricia Arquette stars as a woman plagued by visions of the dead, who uses her unique gift to solve murder cases. In the first episode, her husband Joe gives descriptions of her dreams to law enforcement agencies across the country - and one of the images bears a resemblance to a homicide in Texas. However, she comes into conflict with a cynical ranger who is sceptical about her ability

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: The DA asks Allison to use her powers to select a jury for the trial of a man accused of murder and rape - to ensure those chosen will endorse the use of the death penalty. However, as she studies the case, she becomes convinced the defendant is innocent

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Allison dreams about a husband and wife who die in suspicious circumstances and discovers that her vision may be useful to one of Devalos's colleagues. However, she finds he is not very receptive to her methods. Patricia Arquette stars

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Allison tells Devalos she heard a witness lying to a police sketch artist to implicate an innocent man. Meanwhile, Bridgette's new playmate suggests another family member may be able to see the dead. Paranormal drama, starring Patricia Arquette

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The son of an ex-con under Terry's supervision is arrested for involvement in a protection racket, but is later found beaten semi-conscious by a gang of heavies. Roger reaches rock bottom

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Abra continues to have flashbacks from his time spent working in the Congo, and his situation is not helped by a difficult patient and his daughter. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Lara's nerve is tested when she is confronted by a life-threatening situation, and in the ensuing commotion Harry and Tally patch up their problems. Merlin is devastated by a revelation from Anna, while Eddie develops post-operative septicaemia and a distraught Jack has to face the possibility that his father might die

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Max Little is pushed to the limit by his ex-wife's involvement with big-time gangster Barry Green, and Leela bonds with a busker who has been found beaten and robbed in the park, but faces a dilemma when he turns out to be an illegal immigrant. programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: A distraught Dr Napier berates herself for failing to diagnose a case of diphtheria, while Dr Cameron shoulders the melancholy task of attending an old friend who is hovering at death's door. Drama, starring David Rintoul

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Joanna is presented with an unenviable dilemma by old flame Martin Henderson, who asks her to leave Cardale and start a new life with him. Meanwhile, an elderly lady reluctant to seek help gives Sam cause for concern. Medical drama, starring Haydn Gwynne, Christian Burgess and Joseph Millson

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James and Helen are on their honeymoon and start the tuberculin testing, while Siegfried helps Tristan prepare for his final exams in Edinburgh

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: The Antiques Roadshow comes to town, so William and Hester take along their Chinese vase to get a valuation. On their arrival back home the pair surprise a burglar, so Hester tries out her newly acquired martial arts skills. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Diana is persuaded to broaden her horizons, so she ventures into politics and decides to stand as a candidate for the Residents' Party. Unfortunately, Tom and Harvey ruin her plans by running against her. Stephanie Cole, Graham Crowden and Daniel Hill star

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: The British airmen get stuck down the drain in the village square, and Rene plans to rescue them using an old ice-cream van. As a result, General Von Klinkerhoffen gets more than he bargained for when he decides to buy one of the cool refreshments on sale. Wartime comedy, starring Gorden Kaye

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Billy Hardcastle, also known as the Hawk, claims to be a descendant of Robin Hood - but doesn't share his illustrious ancestor's flair for adventure. Peter Sallis and Keith Clifford star

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: An old friend asks the detective to investigate the death of his father, which he is convinced should be treated as murder. Due to his suspension from the police force, Wallander refuses to get involved - but finds himself drawn back to the case when his acquaintance is also found dead. Drama, starring Kenneth Branagh and Tom Hiddleston, with Rupert Graves, Angela Thorne and Vincent Regan

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: In a special edition of the detective drama that features only two members of the team, Gerry Standing and Steve McAndrew travel to Glasgow, where a new Ucos section is being set up. While there, they agree to assist the investigation into the 1993 unsolved murder of James Soutar, the wealthy owner of a string of betting shops. Cathy Sinclair, one of the beneficiaries of the bookie's will at his death, had a care-home upbringing in common with Soutar, but she claims to have no idea who he was or why she was left £15,000. McAndrew has a personal stake in the case - the original investigating officer was corrupt cop Frank McNair, who had an affair with his wife, and their paths soon cross. The London-based officers' questions begin to unsettle someone and a campaign of victimisation against them begins, but it's a revelation from McAndrew's girlfriend Charley that reawakens a long-dormant scandal and clears the path to a solution. Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: A rock star and his supermodel wife, whose careers are dwindling, report the kidnapping of their baby. Adam is assigned to investigate, but soon discovers the parents are not telling him the whole truth and may even have engineered the situation to boost their flagging stardom. Andy Serkis, who played Gollum in The Lord of the Rings films, guest stars as the desperate musician, alongside Arabella Weir and Tim McInnerny

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Nikki, Jack, Thomas and Clarissa are just settling down for their respective Christmas Day celebrations when they are called in to investigate a shooting spree at an enormous property owned by Andy McMorris, the CEO of a haulage company. Emilia Fox stars

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: A clergyman and his two sisters take a heartbroken Jane under their wing after finding her alone and starving on the moors. The governess embarks on a new life as a parish teacher, but thinks she hears Rochester call out her name and feels compelled to return to Thornfield - where the secrets of the North Tower are finally revealed. Starring Ruth Wilson and Toby Stephens

Mrs Tan safely gives birth to her conjoined twins, but at the same time a journalist poses as a patient to gather information about the family

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jack is horrified to learn the only thing keeping his father alive is the life-support machine - and is quick to vent his anger on Tony. Colette is also taken aback by bad news when a routine antenatal scan reveals her baby has a club foot, prompting fears her unborn infant has other abnormalities. Merlin has difficulty coping with Anna after her shocking revelation and confesses to Lyn that she has ruined his life, while an alcoholic patient gets caught up in the dispute between Jane and Simon. Gabrielle Glaister guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A suspect in an abduction case makes his getaway on board a bus and pulls a gun on Yvonne. The ensuing hostage situation has alarming repercussions for John Heaton, who discovers he's not the only one who's been playing away. Matt tells Emma the church they have booked has had a cancellation and they can get married in three weeks, while Terry hears wonderful news from his ex-wife

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: The medic temporarily hangs up his stethoscope to intervene in a confrontation between Irish farm labourers and irate locals who blame them for a typhus outbreak, resolving matters with a combination of tough talking and common sense. David Rintoul stars, with Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Sam receives a surprise visit from an old friend who seems to have gone up in the world since their schooldays together, but the reunion has a tragic outcome, making him question his skills as a doctor. Meanwhile, Andrew and Dawn return from their trip to the States. Medical drama, starring Joseph Millson and Gary Mavers

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A farmer faces the loss of his savings as his livestock falls victim to a seemingly incurable disease, and James is powerless to help, while Tristan returns from school and admits to James that his exams may not have gone all that well

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester arranges a short cross-Channel trip to France to help take William's mind off his financial worries - and the pair end up shopping in Boulogne for duty free goods. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Diana takes matters into her own hands, asking Tom for sleeping pills after falling ill and fearing the worst. Stephanie Cole stars

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Michelle's cunning plan to release the airmen from the drain in the square is ruined when the gypsy fair is cancelled. Second World War comedy, starring Gorden Kaye and Kirsten Cooke

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard has an unusual gift for Marina's garden but must deliver it without Pearl finding out, and turns to his friends for help. Long-running Pennines comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Robert Fyfe and Jean Fergusson

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Mallory is forced to turn to the sleuthing priest for help when a senior member of the police force is murdered at a bowls match. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Maisie Raine
programme.desc: A girl's rooftop suicide attempt prompts Maisie to reopen inquiries into a murder committed six years previously - but her findings have uncomfortable repercussions closer to home. Pauline Quirke, Steve John Shepherd and Anna Patrick star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Strickland's Whitehall pal Stephen Fisher is targeted by an assassin, so the Ucos boss enlists the team to find whoever was responsible. He reveals that 30 years earlier, he and Fisher were involved in a covert MI5 operation to break into the house of a journalist believed to have information on an IRA arms deals. But with several of the original agents having died in suspicious circumstances, he believes someone is out to kill them all - and when a connection is made between a gangster and MI5, it seems the threat may be coming from within the security service itself. Tim McInnerny and Kerry Fox guest star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The team apprehend an infamous mercenary involved in the arms trade after he is seen visiting an abandoned Soviet weapons dump. Under questioning, he reveals that plans have been put in motion to launch a devastating missile attack on London. Drama, starring David Oyelowo

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The trial of a child murderer Pascoe investigated collapses, and the development has a significant impact on his state of mind. As he struggles to deal with the news, the detective becomes increasingly embroiled in the lives of one of the victim's families, against his partner's advice. Drama, starring Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan

programme.title: Mercy Street
programme.desc: Ridley Scott's historical drama played out against the backdrop of the divisive mid-19th century, as Americans were pitted against each other and families were broken by the Civil War. In the opening episode, New England nurse Mary Phinney begins her duties in Mansion House, a hotel-turned-Union hospital in an occupied Southern town. Mansion House owner James Snr struggles to preserve the family fortune as daughter Emma secretly searches for her beau. Mary Elizabeth Winstead, Josh Radnor and Gary Cole star

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester arranges a short cross-Channel trip to France to help take William's mind off his financial worries - and the pair end up shopping in Boulogne for duty free goods. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.desc: As a grief-stricken Vic and Lucy struggle to come to terms with Peggy's death, life goes on at the health centre, where a man turns nasty when his wife refuses to continue her IVF treatment. Meanwhile, Karen joins the team, but isn't exactly welcomed with open arms, while her husband David starts work at the Goddard's. Rural drama, starring Lesley Dunlop and Leslie Ash.

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Tension mounts between Karen and Jacqui, and an elderly patient confides in Chris, while David comes t o Henry's aid at Goddard's - much to Simon's displeasure. Elsewhere, a patient suspected of having cancer tries to persuade his daughter to accept the new love in his life

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe investigates a spate of garden thefts, focusing his inquiries on a horticultural landscaper whose business has blossomed since the robberies began. Unfortunately, his initial stake-out proves a disaster and, to top it all, Margaret hires the chief suspect to redesign the plot behind the restaurant. Drama, starring Richard Griffiths and Maggie Steed

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe has his hands full again, with Fisher breathing down his neck over an operation to seize stolen vehicles, and a fussy food inspector causing a stir at the restaurant. Crime drama, guest starring Phyllis Logan, with Richard Griffiths and Maggie Steed

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Learning Napoleon has returned from exile, Sharpe leaves Lucille to look after their farm and returns to service on the Prince of Orange's staff. It isn't long before he's reunited with the remaining Chosen Men for one of the most momentous military engagements of all time - the Battle of Waterloo. Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley, Hugh Fraser and Cecile Paoli star

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: An alien clairvoyant dispatches Donna to a world where she never met the Doctor. Only Rose Tyler can help restore the timeline, but as the pair join forces, they hold the fate of all creation in their hands and the temp from Chiswick must face her greatest fears. Catherine Tate, David Tennant and Billie Piper star

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the Earth goes missing, the Time Lord and Donna brave the Shadow Proclamation in an attempt to discover the truth, but a terrifying old enemy waits in the background. Starring David Tennant, Catherine Tate, Billie Piper, Freema Agyeman and John Barrowman

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. The fate of the universe hangs in the balance as the Daleks activate their masterplan. With the Time Lord and Rose imprisoned, and the Tardis seemingly destroyed, the only hope of survival lies with the Doctor's secret army of companions. But as they join forces to battle Davros, Dalek Caan's prophecy declares one of them will die. Starring David Tennant, Catherine Tate and Billie Piper

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Green-fingered amateur detectives Laura and Rosemary are called in to tackle a stubborn species of weed threatening an entire year's vintage at the Crickle Valley vineyard. However, they find themselves in the right place at the right time when the body of a wine critic is discovered on the premises. Murder mystery, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Crime author Jack Rudd visits Brokenwood to give a reading at the local book club - but he never leaves, falling victim to a method of murder portrayed in his own book

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: Phryne arrives at Madame Fleuri's fashion house for a fitting, but unexpectedly finds herself in the middle of a crime scene. Essie Davis stars in the mystery drama set in 1920s Melbourne

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Nikki and the Lyell unit are called in to investigate an attack against a transgender man. Two further assaults are reported, one leaving a potential witness.

programme.desc: The day of Chantelle's funeral arrives, and as family and friends gather to say goodbye, Gray struggles to hold it together. Whitney is about to face her first day in court for the death of Leo, with Sonia there for support - but neither of them are prepared for what happens next. Phil visits Raymond in hospital, feeling an instant bond with the boy, while Jean warns Kush about the risks of online gambling

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: A busy Friday sees almost 700 party-mad people checking in. But with just minutes to go, there are problems with the plumbing. It's even worse for Charley, who has arrived for a hen party organised by her brother, as there are problems with their room - and general manager Damian struggles to placate them. Meanwhile, owners Laurence and Katie throw an extravagant circus-themed party for the twins' fourth birthday - and all the staff are invited

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at the celebrity specials, which saw famous faces swapping showbusiness for big business to raise more than £2million for charity. Highlights include Karren Brady's debut on the show, heading up the women's team in the first celebrity special back in 2007, Jack Dee and Jonathan Ross attempting to design toys, and the moment Omid Djalili lost a carefully placed balloon - not to mention his dignity - as part of Rylan Clark-Neal's fundraising cabaret show

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week. On the panel are Gillian Keegan MP, Minister for Apprenticeships and Skills, Conservative; Andy Burnham, Mayor of Greater Manchester and former Health Secretary, Labour; Dame Donna Kinnair, General Secretary and Chief Executive of the Royal College of Nursing; Yanis Varoufakis, economist, author and former Finance Minister of Greece; and Michael Portillo, television presenter and former Conservative MP and Defence Secretary

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present the weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Cycling legend Chris Hoy, Winter Olympic skeleton gold medallist Amy Williams, former England cricketer Matthew Hoggard and Great Britain 400m runner Perri Shakes-Drayton join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast

A West Midlands police officer returns to work after an assault, only to be attacked again just weeks later. Plus, a West Yorkshire police officer starts his retirement needing physiotherapy after being attacked

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Glasgow, the Special Operations Team races to locate a man who has got into difficulty while swimming in the Clyde, and another who has gone missing in water. Also in Glasgow, a moped rider crashes into a fence and needs treatment for a suspected broken leg. In the countryside, a fisherman has had a stroke in a remote location and needs to be rescued by the Special Operations Team using their special offroad vehicle.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: The Indian Doctor

Rev Todd struggles to maintain his authority and locks away his daughter from prying eyes, while Megan makes a difficult decision. Comedy drama, starring Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their culinary journey through time as they celebrate British food. There is nothing like a hearty breakfast to start the day, but this vital morning meal has continued to change and evolve through time

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to the Scottish Borders with a couple who want their £450,000 budget to buy them an equestrian property in the country. Jules also visits the dramatic ruins of Melrose Abbey, where the heart of Scotland's medieval king Robert the Bruce is believed to be buried

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The return of the makeover show, with Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich designing gardens for a man in Southampton, who has a £5,000 budget for a party space to entertain his friends, but which must also have a feeling of his Welsh homeland about it

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Ninety-three-year-old D-Day veteran Thomas Hassell brings along a treasured item with a proud history - a smart military swagger stick awarded to him while on army service in Berlin for consistently being the smartest soldier on parade. The stick is a cherished symbol of a long and proud career, but sadly the decades have not been kind, and the silver head is badly dented and discoloured. It's down to silversmith Brenton West to knock out the dents and give the stick its swagger back. The team also restores a miniature whisky barrel and an antique set of scales

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Green Party

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Harry Judd present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker, Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by former footballer Dion Dublin, three-time BMX world champion Shanaze Reade, Olympic athlete Iwan Thomas and England rugby union winger Marland Yarde

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by actors Kevin Bishop and Brian Blessed, comedian Josh Widdicombe and historian Kate Williams, who aim to help the team captains deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Whitney is shaken from yesterday's courtroom debacle but Sonia reassures her to put it all behind her, especially as Mick is testifying today. Karen is broken from the funeral, so Mitch makes her an offer. Kat is desperate for money to pay Suki's increased rent - and when Kush comes up trumps, Jean cannot help but be suspicious

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and presenter Adil Ray hosts the satirical quiz, inviting comedian and writer Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Conservative MP Nicky Morgan to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes needs someone to look after Grandad so she can treat herself to a much-needed holiday - but with private nursing homes being so expensive, it seems her only option is to convince the health visitor he has lost his marbles. Rory and Dino move in for a couple of weeks, and the matriarch is soon spitting feathers when the latter begins taking over the house, and her late husband gets in touch from beyond the grave with some ominous news. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host reviews trending news stories before chatting to guests, both in the studio and remotely. Strictl head judge Shirley Ballas discusses her autobiography Behind the Sequins, Hollywood star Ewan McGregor discusses being back on the road for his latest travelogue Long Way Up, dancer and presenter Ashley Banjo promotes the new Diversity tour Connected, while music superstar Miley Cyrus chats and performs her current single Midnight Sky. Plus, members of a reduced-size audience try to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Nora takes a job at a real estate office and becomes a hyper-competent assistant with a little help from Adderall, which alienates her online gaming friends in the process. Comedy, starring Awkwafina

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Nora attempts to cash a cheque to pick her car up from an impound lot. However, her problems mount as she discovers that her bank has closed her account. Comedy, starring Awkwafina

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at the celebrity specials, which saw famous faces swapping showbusiness for big business to raise more than £2million for charity. Highlights include Karren Brady's debut on the show, heading up the women's team in the first celebrity special back in 2007, Jack Dee and Jonathan Ross attempting to design toys, and the moment Omid Djalili lost a carefully placed balloon - not to mention his dignity - as part of Rylan Clark-Neal's fundraising cabaret show

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Jason Atherton and special guest Vic Reeves. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain focuses on something close to her heart - baking delicious treats to share with loved ones. Nadiya comes from a large family, so baking for others has always taken centre stage in her own home. She kicks things off with her chilli and cranberry brioche wreath, featuring fluffy indulgent dough balls surrounding an entire baked camembert cheese, creating the ultimate tear and share. Over the years, Nadiya has become an expert in catering for big family gatherings, and she has learnt that there is nothing better for impressing guests than a gigantic stack of delicious desserts. Her mini pecan pies filled with clotted cream ensure that everyone has their own little parcel of perfection. Next up is a cheat's bread-and-butter pudding, using ice-cream and shop-bought croissants to slash the traditional cooking time without compromising on flavour

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's Nations League fixtures, including England v Belgium and Republic of Ireland v Wales

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up with a development he first saw in Anthorn, Cumbria in 2012, and the team follows projects in Hounslow, west London, and Crewe in Cheshire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, where experts Mark Stacey and Raj Bisram help competing teams bag the best buys to make a profit at auction

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps couple looking for a home in Ceredigion on a budget of £355,000, and also heads to the small town of Llandysul to meet a dynamic dairy farming family

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms three items from the Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey, including a collection of fishing rods, and a reproduction designer chair

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie and the Rich brother's face one of their hardest ever garden rescues to date in Stowmarket, turning a concrete space into a family friendly back garden

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special family edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants Marcus Brigstocke, Helen Brigstocke, David Seaman, Frankie Seaman, Naughty Boy, Aisha Khan, Toby Tarrant and Fia Tarrant take part

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are mother and daughter Shirley and Siobhan from London, radio DJs and best friends Mike and Brian from Glasgow, and engaged couple Rachel and Raj from Hampshire

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: Little Mix band members Perrie, Jesy, Leigh-Anne and Jade search for a female dance group, with the all-singing, all-dancing hopefuls auditioning individually before being tested alongside each other, with only best making the final line up and going through to the performance shows

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: In the final episode, Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman, the judges and the show's professional dancers look back at some of the most memorable moments from Strictly Come Dancing finals, reminiscing about some of the most emotional and nerve-wracking nights inside the Strictly ballroom. This special episode also pays tribute to the 2014 Strictly champion, Caroline Flack

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the second episode, Jack and Michaela, a young couple from Wigan, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Captain America: The Winter Soldier (2014)
programme.desc: The superhuman soldier becomes an operative for secret intelligence agency SHIELD, but grows suspicious of their motives. Following a battle with a mysterious cyborg assassin, he and fellow Avenger Black Widow become fugitives, and try to bring down a sinister conspiracy. Comic-book thriller sequel, starring Chris Evans, Scarlett Johansson, Samuel L Jackson and Robert Redford

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game between Chicago Bears and Tampa Bay Buccaneers. Plus, a look at the major news stories shaping the American football world, as the leading sides in the NFL continue their bids to succeed Kansas City Chiefs as Super Bowl-winners

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a recently retired couple find a dream country home in the counties of Shropshire and Herefordshire, and also visits the Weston Park Model Air Show

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A police officer shot at close range is in danger of losing his sight, and a speeding motorcyclist is pursued through London

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: The farmers prepare for the worst of the winter weather, and disaster strikes for Helen and Graham when one of their Highland cows contracts a chronic, contagious wasting disease. In the Northumberland National Park, Emma and Ewan prepare for a sheepdog trial, while in Cumbria, Frank and his daughter Georgia acquire a small herd of pygmy goats. Joyce travels to Edinburgh to give a speech to mark the launch of the Women in Agriculture report at the Royal Highland and Agriculture headquarters

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: Quiz show in which a team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: An antiques expert, a market trader and an upcycler try to make the most money for BBC Children in Need, beginning with Melissa Downhill, Kevin Paxton and Arusha Irvine

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George struggles to cope after Shay leaves. Meanwhile, the whole of Weld camp out on Big Mac's beach side field to protest his plans to build on it

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The competition reaches its climax as the three finalists enter the sewing room for the last time, with Esme Young and Patrick Grant setting them three challenges based on evening wear. They must first demonstrate their precision by creating a man's pin-tucked dress shirt and then have to transform a tuxedo into a little black dress in the alteration task. Finally they are asked to produce a floor-length evening gown for a model of their choice, before the judges decide which of them wins the title. Claudia Winkleman presents

programme.title: The Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan returns to Minnesota in the summer to catch up with the black bears, but is upset to discover that cub Hope has been abandoned by her mother. With the young animal facing starvation, he is forced to step in and become a surrogate parent, and balances his new duties with continued study of the rest of the family

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Thomas Plant and David Harper are on hand at the Watts Gallery in Surrey to find a selection of objects to take to auction

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Rick Edwards, Scarlett Moffatt, Naga Munchetty and David O'Doherty test their general knowledge skills on this week's special House of Games: Champion editions

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: The fishing boat Reliance spends the day in port, two of its Ghanaian crewmen take a trip into Peterhead to buy gifts for their families back home, while on the Amity, skipper Phil Reid is trying a new fishing ground to see if he can find a decent catch

programme.title: The River Wye with Will Millard
programme.desc: The writer and angler joins a group of young people from across Britain as they take to the water at Glasbury, Powys, to travel 100 miles downriver by canoe and kayak, before he heads to Hay-on-Wye to learn how it reinvented itself as a literary hotspot, now famous for its festival

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Mary analyses the worlds of architecture and fashion during the pandemic, noting that while the clothes people wear have already changed, cities and homes need to change to accommodate new ways of living. She also compares the new normal with life in ancient Rome, and fulfils a 40-year ambition by reuniting three cast members of 1970s BBC drama I, Claudius - Derek Jacobi, Sian Phillips and Brian Blessed

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a cherry Bakewell factory in Stoke on Trent that produces 250,000 tarts every day, and meets the team of 12 employed to precisely place the cherries on top by hand. Cherry Healey learns the tricks of avoiding a soggy pastry bottom when baking at home, and visits a factory where almonds are roasted and milled to make butter. Historian Ruth Goodman discovers the origins of frangipane as a perfume in 17th-century Paris, and discovers how the modern cherry Bakewell recipe was arrived at by accident

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.desc: Police Scotland's major investigation teams deal with around 60 murders a year and this edition delves deeper into the nature of the job. Through reconstructions and interviews with both senior and rookie detectives, the film reveals what it is like to work on such devastating cases as the murder of Alesha MacPhail and the disappearance of Julie Reilly.

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's New World Order
programme.desc: The comedian dissects the week's news with the help of guest panellists Kiri Pritchard-McLean, Sara Pascoe and Sophie Duker

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: A filmic offshoot of Tim Key's stage show Megadate, in which a man dazzles a woman on the perfect first date. However, she doesn't text him back, leading him to retrace his steps. His path is riddled with cynics and naysayers, questioning his optimistic outlook on how the date went. The nerd in the bowling alley who thought he'd embarrassed himself, the old eel catcher on the mud flats trying to douse his dreams with wisdom, and the compassionate waitress at the chicken shop. Starring Tim Key, Mandeep Dhillon, Sam Fletcher, Bill Paterson and Julie Dray

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: David Jenkins, a world-leading surgeon at Royal Papworth, performs one of the most demanding operations possible, draining a patient's body of blood to remove clots in her lungs. At Addenbrooke's hospital, consultant orthopaedic surgeon Andrew Carrothers is also pushing at the boundaries of medical science with a complex operation that he has devised called the Harrington Plus. Frustrated by the lack of options for patients whose pelvis and hip bones have been badly weakened by cancer, Andrew added a crucial stage to an existing operation to enable cancer patients to walk again

programme.title: The Romantics and Us with Simon Schama
programme.desc: The historian recounts the story of how the Romantics - fuelled by the failures of the French Revolution and horrors of the Industrial Revolution - retreated from the tragic uproar of public life to take a journey into their own minds. Here, long before Freud and Jung and the modern psychological revolution, they discovered the subconscious - something that would have a profound effect on the modern world. Contributors include Tobias Menzies, Peter Doherty, Piotr Anderszewski and David Attenborough

programme.title: Extinction: The Facts
programme.desc: With a million species at threat, David Attenborough explores the science of extinction and how this has consequences for everyone. The programme examines why biodiversity loss is happening and what it means - not just for the natural world, but for all mankind. The loss of biodiversity threatens food and water security, undermines humans' ability to control the climate, and places humans at greater risk of pandemics. Leading experts including Sir Robert Watson and plant scientist Prof Kathy Willis explain how all groups in the natural world are in decline, and that extinction is now happening up to 100 times faster than the natural evolutionary rate

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton guides a pair of budding entrepreneurs who want to escape to the Cotswolds. They have a generous £900,000 to play with, but the property must have the potential to develop a boutique country holiday let. Alistair also spends an exhilarating day at the University of Bath as he launches himself on to the UK's only bobsleigh and skeleton push-start track

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A former policeman in Northern Ireland forced to rebuild his life after a rocket attack on his vehicle and a Leeds paramedic threatened by a hospital patient with a 12-inch knife

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a rusty toy pedal car that is missing its steering wheel, a Victorian stamp perforating machine, a bronze statue of a dagger-wielding assassin by leading French artist Claire Colinet, and a baize-covered card table dating from the 1940s that has sentimental value for its owner

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A TV extra, an exam invigilator and a bank clerk compete must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Antiques auctioneer Charles Hanson, top trader Linda Lambert and trendy upcycler Zoe Pocock head to Glastonbury to find some bargains they can turn into some flipping profit. Rummaging through antique shops, scrap yards and flea markets, they only have one day to each find those hidden second-hand or used gems that will make them a nice big profit

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: It is almost Bonfire Night, and Tom is already on Monty's case, insisting that there be no repeat of last year's incident with the fireworks and the surfboard. George is baffled by their enthusiasm, but he has different problems - he is now entangled in a relationship with Jan, and Shay is still in Sydney. Arlo commences Operation Shay to try to tempt her back by pretending they are fine without her

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett hosts as the fifth series of the contest gets under way, with Patrick Grant and Esme Young passing judgement once more. The first pattern challenge is a Dior-inspired 'wiggle dress' designed to hug the female form. Contestants also have to transform three second-hand items of denim clothing into a single, stylish new garment, and the final task involves a made-to-measure jumpsuit

programme.title: The Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan returns to Minnesota at the beginning of autumn to check how abandoned bear cub Hope is progressing and is delighted to discover that her life has taken a turn for the better. However, a fresh danger is posed by the arrival of hunters in the forest and Gordon decides to walk with the animals to protect them

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin visits the picturesque Cotswolds village of Broadway to uncover its artistic past. He also revisits some of the historic venues from the series, with new valuations and unexpected auctions

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Rick Edwards, Scarlett Moffatt, Naga Munchetty and David O'Doherty test their general knowledge skills on this week's special House of Games: Champion editions

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: The Boy John sets out from Peterhead harbour in search of whitefish, travelling for 24 hours to reach the remote Norwegian sector of the North Sea. The boat burns more than £1,000 worth of fuel just to get there, but the catches are poor, leaving skipper Jonny McLeman is feeling the pressure.

programme.title: Simply Nigella
programme.desc: The food writer demonstrates her favourite recipes for sharing with family and friends, including a tray of nourishing breakfast bars, a supper of crunchy chicken cutlets, and a dark and sumptuous chocolate cake. Plus she shows to make succulent belly pork burgers, which were inspired by a visit to a New York eatery

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Presenter Sara Cox is joined by comedians Phil Wang, Sara Pascoe and Tom Allen and singer Sophie Ellis-Bextor. The panellists share their favourite books, revealing those that make them laugh and the fictional characters they would like to meet. They also discuss this week's book group pick, The Midnight Library by Matt Haig and Sara Pascoe's latest book, Sex, Power, Money

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: As winter morphs into spring, lambing and calving keeps the team busy out on the farms. However, their small animal clinic is also packed with pets and Martin Fitzpatrick - the man who founded the practice 35 years ago - faces a difficult case concerning a Highland terrier. Young vet Darren is called out on an emergency to a farm where a prize cow is struggling to deliver a calf. An examination reveals it's simply too big to come out naturally. Darren decides the safest option, for both the cow and the calf, is to perform an emergency Caesarean section. It's a risky operation, but without it, the cow will die

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Newly qualified vet Patrick is called out to help a farmer whose cow has been struggling all night with a difficult labour. Patrick discovers her calf is breached, and has to reposition it. However, once it's in the correct position, a shock discovery makes his job twice as precarious. Back in Castlewellan, a farmer arrives with a two-week-old lamb that's developed a limp. Some antibiotics and a dose of anti-inflammatories will help the lamb back on its feet. However, vet Maeve notices its eyes are milky white and she has to use all her expertise and knowledge to save the lamb's sight

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As autumn gets underway, Monty Don gives advice on what plants need to be taken under cover now, what can stay out until after the first frosts and others that just need an extra layer of protection. Joe Swift visits broadcaster Kate Garraway in her London garden, who discusses how it has been a sanctuary over recent months. Nick Bailey reveals the results of the design project he started in his own garden earlier in the year, while there's another chance to meet a woman who has filled her garden in Kent with exotic and tropical plants

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools welcomes guest Sam Smith, who discusses music, loves and influences. From London, Smith first appeared on Later in 2013 with dance duo Disclosure, making a solo debut the year after. Two number one albums - In the Lonely Hour and The Thrill of It All - followed along with seven number one singles and a heaving awards cabinet of BRITs, Grammys and an Oscar for a James Bond theme. Plus, appearances by Jamaican artist Koffee and folk artist Shirley Collins

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark reviews the day's important national and international news stories

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Adama finds Tigh's wife, who was presumed dead. Laura grows suspicious of Adama, while the reappearance of Col Tigh's wife also raises questions as everybody on board becomes a Cylon suspect. Edward James Olmos stars

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: In the face of a fuel shortage, Commander Adama decides to raid a heavily armed Cylon fuel refinery, and his plan puts Lee and Starbuck's leadership qualities to the test. Meanwhile, Dr Baltar's reputed expertise regarding the enemy means he is called upon for vital information about the target site

programme.title: Southside with You (2016)
programme.desc: Biographical drama chronicling the afternoon in the summer of 1989, when the future 44th President of the United States Barack Obama and Michelle Robinson went on their first date across the South Side of Chicago. Starring Tika Sumpter and Parker Sawyers

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Thirty-four people died in Australia last year as the worst bushfires in living memory swept across the country , with an area roughly the size of England devastated and thousands of homes destroyed. A year on, Clive Myrie hears from the people living in the path of the fires and the firefighters who risked their lives to save them, and asks if these levels of destruction are to become normal

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker looks back on his documentaries about the criminal justice system in the US. Louis reflects on his reports on the prison system, which exposed a world with its own rules and codes and a system that seemed to be broken. He also revisits the documentaries he shot in Philadelphia and Milwaukee, where tensions between the African-American community and the police were already at boiling point years before the death of George Floyd

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood examine a play date with a polar bear, a dolphin that thinks it is a sperm whale, and the ducklings with a cat for a mum

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Featuring Norfolk's World Championship Thumb Wrestling

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: CBBC star Sam Nixon invites viewers into his back garden to make pizza, BGT semi-finalist Yakub performs a dance routine live in the studio and brothers Cam, Tyler and Aaron reveal what it's like to live and work in a zoo as part of their CBBC series One Zoo Three

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia moves into a new apartment with her best friends, Vinny and Poppy

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia encounters a passive-aggressive lift

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve is on a mission to find the weirdest dinosaur ever

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox reveals how the unique properties of light provide an insight into the origins and evolution of both mankind and the universe. He charts the importance of light in the development of the world's ancient civilisations, and explains how the speed of light allows the measurement of both distance and time - before pinpointing one of the most important moments in the evolution of life on Earth

programme.title: Monkey Kingdom (2015)
programme.desc: Documentary following a newborn monkey and its mother as they struggle to survive within the competitive social hierarchy of the Temple Troop, a group of simians who live in ancient ruins found deep in the jungles of Sri Lanka. Narrated by Tina Fey

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: As winter morphs into spring, lambing and calving keeps the team busy out on the farms. However, their small animal clinic is also packed with pets and Martin Fitzpatrick - the man who founded the practice 35 years ago - faces a difficult case concerning a Highland terrier. Young vet Darren is called out on an emergency to a farm where a prize cow is struggling to deliver a calf. An examination reveals it's simply too big to come out naturally. Darren decides the safest option, for both the cow and the calf, is to perform an emergency Caesarean section. It's a risky operation, but without it, the cow will die

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Newly qualified vet Patrick is called out to help a farmer whose cow has been struggling all night with a difficult labour. Patrick discovers her calf is breached, and has to reposition it. However, once it's in the correct position, a shock discovery makes his job twice as precarious. Back in Castlewellan, a farmer arrives with a two-week-old lamb that's developed a limp. Some antibiotics and a dose of anti-inflammatories will help the lamb back on its feet. However, vet Maeve notices its eyes are milky white and she has to use all her expertise and knowledge to save the lamb's sight

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King conclude their culinary journey around Japan by heading into the heart of the country to find out about the historic roots of Japanese cuisine. Dave puts his Strictly Come Dancing footwork into action when kneading noodle dough the artisan way, before the duo take an awe-inspiring bike ride to see Mount Fuji, spend a night at a Buddhist temple and are entertained by a geisha in Kyoto, complete with drinking games

programme.title: Queen of Katwe (2016)
programme.desc: The life of a poor Ugandan youngster is changed for ever when she is introduced to the game of chess. Drama, starring David Oyelowo, Lupita Nyong'o and Madina Nalwanga

programme.title: Extraordinary Animals
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and a team of wildlife experts focus on animals' relationships with each other and with humans. Hugh discovers how the capuchin monkeys of Costa Rica have such a fantastic team spirit, biologist Patrick Aryee investigates man's relationship with the vulture - nature's most infamous scavenger - and zoologist Lucy Cooke finds out about the medicine in Kentucky that may improve a rare pig's relationship problems. Conservationist Giles Clark ventures into the rainforests of Thailand to find out how a clever matchmaking technique could help save the clouded leopard from extinction

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: David Harper and Natasha Raskin search for antiques at Watts Gallery in Surrey, and Paul Martin uncovers the story of the ceramicist and designer Mary Watts

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin revisits his travel series, beginning with Around the World in 80 Days. He draws on his personal archive of audio recordings and diary notebooks, and reflects on how he became a seasoned global traveller. The first episode revisits his visits to Venice, Cairo, Saudi Arabia, India, Hong Kong and China - a nation that was closed to most westerners at the time. Featuring contributions from fans and friends including David Attenborough, Joanna Lumley and Simon Reeve

programme.title: Golf: PGA Championship
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour introduces the key third-round action from the European Tour's traditional flagship event at Wentworth's famous West Course. Reigning champion Danny Willett will be among the British and global stars hoping to still be in contention at this stage of the rescheduled tournament, which is being held without fans because of the coronavirus pandemic. Peter Alliss, Ken Brown and Andrew Cotter provide the commentary

programme.title: Scotland from the Sky
programme.desc: James Crawford explores how planners and architects took to Scotland's skies after the Second World War as they embarked on missions to redesign towns and cities. From building a motorway right through the heart of Glasgow, to one of Scotland's first tower blocks, James reveals how these utopian and controversial visions have transformed lives of people living in Scotland today

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: A look across Hawaii's varied islands, discovering how they were made, and how its wildlife thrives. The most remote island chain on Earth, its tropical shores are hard to reach. However, for the hardy creatures that have made it there, such as the waterfall-climbing fish, carnivorous caterpillar and Laysan albatross, a land of opportunity awaits. From newly formed lava fields, to lush jungles, and vibrant coral reefs, these diverse and beautiful islands have it all. As this documentary reveals, its people are looking to the future and attempting to protect and foster its flora and fauna

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig takes the quiz in a 'ridiculous' direction this evening. Trying to come up with the answers are Alan Davies, Maisie Adam, David Mitchell and Holly Walsh

programme.title: Teddy Pendergrass: If You Don't Know Me
programme.desc: Documentary about the life, music and towering achievements of soul singer Teddy Pendergrass, from his origins and early life in Philadelphia. The film charts his early successes with Harold Melvin & the Blue Notes and his solo superstardom and sex symbol status, before moving on to his later life and continued success after overcoming the car crash that left him paralysed

programme.title: Soul America
programme.desc: Slavery once drove the economy of the American South and when it was finally abolished in 1863, its gospel tradition lived on. As the black American church grew in strength and popularity, so did its singers. A network of performers emerged known as the gospel highway and in the 1950s, soul music's first generation of stars emerged. The genre delivered an assertive, integrated vision of black America during the Civil Rights era of the early 1960s, presided over by the likes of Otis Redding and Aretha Franklin. Narrated by Carleen Anderson

programme.title: Ghost Stories (2017)
programme.desc: Professor Philip Goodman, famous for debunking hoaxes and ghost sightings, is given a dossier of three terrifying, unsolvable cases by his hero and role model Charles Cameron, who has been missing, presumed dead, for years. Can Goodman explain what even Cameron could not? Horror anthology, starring Andy Nyman, Martin Freeman and Paul Whitehouse

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A Garden in Snowdonia
programme.desc: With Bodnant lying under a blanket of snow, head gardener Troy Scott Smith plans a winter-themed display to attract more visitors. Plus, a 300-year-old oak succumbs to age, and moles wreak havoc on the team's preparations for the new season

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a lost little cabin and an ice-covered lake surrounded by a snowy mountain range

programme.title: The Searchers (1956)
programme.desc: An embittered American Civil War veteran sets out to find his niece, who has been abducted by a renegade Comanche tribe. His search takes him many years, and during the long quest his travelling companion starts to wonder if he intends to rescue her or kill her. John Ford's Western, starring John Wayne, Natalie Wood, Jeffrey Hunter and Vera Miles

programme.title: How the Wild West Was Won with Ray Mears
programme.desc: The adventurer explores how the American wilderness shaped the Wild West. He begins by looking at the landscapes of three great mountain ranges - the Appalachians, the Rockies and the Sierra Nevada - and how they challenged the westward push of early pioneers. He discovers how their geography, extreme weather, wild animals and ecology provided both opportunities and challenges for those living there, and also experiences mule-trekking and gold prospecting

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: The creation of the band's second album, which took the gothic synth-pop foundations of the band and combined them with arena-ready anthems leading to critical acclaim and the international hit singles Mothers Talk, Shout and Everybody Wants to Rule the World. With full co-operation of the band members, this documentary explores how the album was recorded, leaving its indelible imprint on New Wave

programme.title: David Stratton's Stories of Australian Cinema
programme.desc: How variations on the theme of family are depicted in Australian movie-making The Castle's nuclear, if unorthodox family, a family of faith in The Devil's Playground, Romper Stomper's frightening Neo-Nazis and crime families such as those depicted in Ned Kelly and Animal Kingdom

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a lost little cabin and an ice-covered lake surrounded by a snowy mountain range

programme.title: A Garden in Snowdonia
programme.desc: With Bodnant lying under a blanket of snow, head gardener Troy Scott Smith plans a winter-themed display to attract more visitors. Plus, a 300-year-old oak succumbs to age, and moles wreak havoc on the team's preparations for the new season

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane - at least for a while - to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane -to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.title: How the Wild West Was Won with Ray Mears
programme.desc: The adventurer explores how the American wilderness shaped the Wild West. He begins by looking at the landscapes of three great mountain ranges - the Appalachians, the Rockies and the Sierra Nevada - and how they challenged the westward push of early pioneers. He discovers how their geography, extreme weather, wild animals and ecology provided both opportunities and challenges for those living there, and also experiences mule-trekking and gold prospecting

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Gary Davies presents the edition first aired on January 4, featuring performances by the Quireboys, Sonia and Latino Rave

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Simon Mayo presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 11 January 1990 and featuring Deacon Blue, New Kids on the Block and the Mission

programme.title: A Hard Day's Night (1964)
programme.desc: The Beatles, harassed by their manager and Paul's grandfather, embark on a train journey to London for a TV show, but find themselves dogged by all manner of problems, including the inevitable overexcited fans. Musical comedy, starring the Fab Four alongside Wilfrid Brambell, Victor Spinetti and Anna Quayle. Songs include Can't Buy Me Love, Don't Bother Me and the title track

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Another chance to see Steve Wright present a special edition first broadcast in 2000 to mark what would have been John Lennon's 60th year. Featured songs include Imagine, Jealous Guy, Instant Karma, Mind Games and Stand By Me, and the programme includes an interview with Yoko Ono

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: The story of John Lennon and Yoko Ono's move to New York City in 1971, as the singer sought to escape the mayhem of the Beatles era and focus on his private life. Alan Yentob charts the ups and downs the couple faced - including their battle against the immigration services and Lennon's infamous 'lost weekend', when he left Yoko for their personal assistant and disappeared to LA for more than a year. The film features interviews with Ono, photographer Bob Gruen and Elton John, as well as studio recordings from the Double Fantasy sessions, concert out-takes and home-movie clips

programme.title: The Beatles: Made on Merseyside
programme.desc: Few dispute that the Beatles defined 1960s music and popular culture like no other band, but how John, Paul, George and Ringo made the journey from Merseyside teenagers to international pop stars is less known. Director Alan Byron's documentary recounts the rise of the band as American rock 'n' roll and rhythm and blues dragged post-war Liverpool into one of the most vibrant music cities ever with the Mersey Beat sound

programme.title: Sings the Beatles
programme.desc: A look back through the BBC's archives at memorable covers of classic Beatles songs. Included are a performance of Day Tripper by Sandie Shaw, Shirley Bassey's version of Something, the Carpenters' rendition of Help!, Oasis's reinvention of I Am the Walrus, and Joe Cocker's chart-topping interpretation of With a Little Help from My Friends. Plus, Paul McCartney's solo performance of Lady Madonna

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Musicians Midge Ure and Kim Appleby visit London and Manchester, the two cities that did battle with each other for musical pre-eminence as '80s music turned towards the new sounds of dance. This is a tale of how studio technology changed music - with British bands putting their own unique spin on dance to produce contrasting northern and southern sounds. Contributors include Denise Pearson from Five Star, Soul II Soul's Jazzie B, Mark Moore of S-Express, Shaun Ryder from the Happy Mondays and Peter Hook of New Order

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Music from the height of the hippie era, featuring Manfred Mann, Herman's Hermits, Julie Driscoll and Brian Auger, the Alan Price Set, the Crazy World of Arthur Brown and the Rolling Stones.

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Another chance to see Steve Wright present a special edition first broadcast in 2000 to mark what would have been John Lennon's 60th year. Featured songs include Imagine, Jealous Guy, Instant Karma, Mind Games and Stand By Me, and the programme includes an interview with Yoko Ono

programme.title: Francesco's Venice
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto charts the changes that took place as Venice became increasingly prosperous, marking the city's great age of trading, while artists such as Titian, Veronese and Tintoretto heralded a golden era of Venetian painting. Merchant princes began building resplendent palaces along the Grand Canal, only for disaster to strike in 1575 as plague hit the city, killing almost a third of the population

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The intrepid explorer sets off from Little Diomede Island in the Bering Strait on an epic 50,000-mile trek around the Pacific Rim. On the first stage of his journey across Russia, he travels through the notorious Kolyma region via the hot springs of Kamchatka. He then makes a guest appearance with the Russian Pacific Fleet Song and Dance Ensemble to sing one of Stalin's favourite patriotic anthems

programme.title: Inspector Montalbano
programme.desc: It is high summer in Vigata and the detective investigates when the body of Agata Cosentino is found in the municipal archives where she worked. But the building was supposed to be closed for the holidays, so what was the young woman doing there? Italian crime drama, starring Luca Zingaretti

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: A prominent Danish gender campaigner is found dead in Malmo, meaning that Swedish detective Saga Noren must once again collaborate with a counterpart in the Danish police as she investigates. With Martin Rohde now in gaol for murder, Saga is assigned a new colleague in Copenhagen, but the pair's working relationship fails to get off to a good start, a fact only exacerbated by Saga's role in getting well-liked Danish cop Martin sent down. Starring Sofia Helin and Kirsten Olesen. In Swedish and Danish

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Malmo detective Saga and her Copenhagen colleague Hanne must put aside their differences to catch the perpetrator of the so-called 'Clown Murders'. A prime suspect arrives in the form of the first victim's apparently traumatised son, who has gone on the run. Meanwhile, Saga is distracted from her investigation by the untimely arrival of her mother, who insists on showing her a medical file that she hopes will put an end to their estrangement. Crime drama, in Swedish and Danish, starring Sofia Helin and Kirsten Olesen

programme.desc: Issie gets a new vision of Mystic running through the forest along an old railway track, and wonders if this might be the clue that will lead her to Blaze’s herd. As they journey deeper into the forest, past their favourite swimming hole, the kids make a series of bewildering discoveries and eventually realise they’re completely and utterly lost.
