programme.desc: When a relative suggests that Mali will never go to space he becomes a bit sad. However, his friends set out to teach Mali he can be anything he wants to be

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat: Rockpool Tales
programme.desc: Veteran seafarer Old Jack tells a story to his faithful companion Salty dog about Belinda the Blenny, who wants to know where the twinkles on the sea come from

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm Huntley goes to a farm in Yorkshire to find out all about goats and visits a big autumn show to see how harvest time is celebrated. JB Gill gets busy picking juicy plums and finds out what we can do to make them last all year round

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.desc: In this programme we visit three children who are all having a fascinating day out. In Russia, seven-year-old Liza picks blueberries with her grandma - and helps turn them into delicious pancakes. In Ghana, Tyra, who's five, goes to the market with her mum and learns how to do the shopping. And in Jordan, Hamza, who's eight, has a day out in town with his dad, and buys a lovely present for his baby sister, Mimi. We see that although children live in different countries, may look different and speak different languages, the joys and discoveries of childhood are universal.

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers have fun on a walk to a pumpkin patch!

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Rory visits Amber at a riding stable to meet Trigger and the other ponies

programme.title: Monty & Co
programme.desc: Lulu trusts Monty to babysit her dolls, but he finds that it's not as easy as it looks when the dolls go missing.

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: When the Olobobs go out for a picnic, big clouds cover the sky and it starts raining

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble is counting fish with Fisherman Tumble, while Justin and his friends are counting animals at a wildlife park

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: Andy travels back 65 million years to the time of the Tyrannosaurus Rex to record the sound of its roar, but it means getting up close to the predator

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie visits a factory where handwash is made and uses her special camera to see inside a big vat to see the colour being mixed

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Mr McGregor fills a bird table with sunflower seeds, Squirrel Nutkin becomes dangerously obsessed with raiding it

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: A race between Xuli, Lars and Glitch gets serious when a tornado hits the Academy Island.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo is excited to show Gran Gran around the city farm - it's been a few months since she was last there, and she can't wait to see Melanie the chick again. But when they arrive at the chicken enclosure, they can't find the little fluffy chick - where could she be?

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: When Jess takes Evie for her first school visit, Waffle goes along too - on the condition that he doesn't sing. He tries his best to behave, but it's hard for a Wonder Dog!

programme.title: My Pet and Me
programme.desc: Rory Crawford heads to a community farm to meet some children who enjoy spending time there with all the different animals

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers go on a walk to play with pinwheels in the wind.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: When Mo's kite gets stuck up on an office roof, the team rescue it in a down-to-earth way

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: Kari hosts a talent show to demonstrate the Treasure Champs' many skills, but Barry needs some help in recognising that his unique talent is worthy of celebrating

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: The story of how the ants learned to work together

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie Moate finds out how clocks tell the time, heading to a clock factory and using a special time-lapse camera to show how the hands on the clock move around

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: But even though it's sunny when they leave the house, a rainy weather forecast threatens her plans. Luckily for her, Gran Gran has come prepared!

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: With her parents stay at the hospital on the mainland awaiting the delivery of the new baby, Katie looks after the family cat, Fabbydoo, but he mysteriously disappears

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: When the zip on Sarah's hoodie breaks, Scarf Lady suggests a trip to the Haber Dasher to get it fixed

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: Lola resolves to learn to listen after her inattention gets her into trouble both at home and at school

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee wakes up super-early and wants to play immediately. Mo wonders how much zooming is needed before everybody needs a nap

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: The Iron Chicken's radio breaks down, so she and the Wols create their own radio station

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos explore the house - right to the top of the stairs and then back down again

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Adjoa Andoh reads I Love You Night and Day, by Smriti Prasadam-Halls

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Louise Minchin and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela meets fed-up pedestrians who want to bring an end to motorists parking on the pavement. They say it is dangerous and inconsiderate - drivers say they have no choice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Lucy Alexander follow the progress of properties in the London borough of Camden, Coatbridge in North Lanarkshire and Bootle on Merseyside

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A former policeman in Northern Ireland forced to rebuild his life after a rocket attack on his vehicle and a Leeds paramedic threatened by a hospital patient with a 12-inch knife

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: An accident on the Isle of Mull leaves a man seriously injured, triggering a response from the air ambulance. A tourist in Edinburgh has breathing problems

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Raj Bisram help two teams scour an antiques fair in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, while Natasha Raskin Sharp sets a challenge for some other people attending

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: The quarantine continues and the supplies start getting low, and Prem is no wiser about the source of the virus. Megan and Emlyn come up with a plan to get more food from the next village, but events take a turn when Mary arrives with terrible news

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the British tradition of cooking with food hunted from the land and prepare a selection of dishes using rabbit, grouse and venison

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple looking for a property in Gloucestershire on a budget of £850,000 and visits an ice cream-making family on Rodburgh Common

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Oxfordshire mum Sarah is after a space where she can relax and unwind after a full-on day minding two young children, but is also aware that the garden also needs to work as a play space for the youngsters. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Bicester to assist Sarah and partner Adam, who need a garden the whole family can play and relax in

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team tackle a shattered ceramic dish, a hand-made doll's house and a vintage harmonium that provided the soundtrack to many an East End knees-up

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The day of Chantelle's funeral arrives, and as family and friends gather to say goodbye, Gray struggles to hold it together. Whitney is about to face her first day in court for the death of Leo, with Sonia there for support - but neither of them are prepared for what happens next. Phil visits Raymond in hospital, feeling an instant bond with the boy, while Jean warns Kush about the risks of online gambling

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: A busy Friday sees almost 700 party-mad people checking in. But with just minutes to go, there are problems with the plumbing. It's even worse for Charley, who has arrived for a hen party organised by her brother, as there are problems with their room - and general manager Damian struggles to placate them. Meanwhile, owners Laurence and Katie throw an extravagant circus-themed party for the twins' fourth birthday - and all the staff are invited

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at the celebrity specials, which saw famous faces swapping showbusiness for big business to raise more than £2million for charity. Highlights include Karren Brady's debut on the show, heading up the women's team in the first celebrity special back in 2007, Jack Dee and Jonathan Ross attempting to design toys, and the moment Omid Djalili lost a carefully placed balloon - not to mention his dignity - as part of Rylan Clark-Neal's fundraising cabaret show

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week. On the panel are Gillian Keegan MP, Minister for Apprenticeships and Skills, Conservative; Andy Burnham, Mayor of Greater Manchester and former Health Secretary, Labour; Dame Donna Kinnair, General Secretary and Chief Executive of the Royal College of Nursing; Yanis Varoufakis, economist, author and former Finance Minister of Greece; and Michael Portillo, television presenter and former Conservative MP and Defence Secretary

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present the weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Cycling legend Chris Hoy, Winter Olympic skeleton gold medallist Amy Williams, former England cricketer Matthew Hoggard and Great Britain 400m runner Perri Shakes-Drayton join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: How one of the biggest online shopping companies in the world has left its customers wishing they had taken their business elsewhere

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Wednesfield, Wolverhampton, Sydenham in south London and Chatham in Kent, then talk to the new owners

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A spate of burglaries at Christmas leads to a police community support officer and his colleague being attacked, as they track down and try to arrest the thief. A West Midlands police officer returns to work after an assault, only to be attacked again just weeks later. Plus, a West Yorkshire police officer starts his retirement needing physiotherapy after being attacked

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Glasgow, the Special Operations Team races to locate a man who has got into difficulty while swimming in the Clyde, and another who has gone missing in water. Also in Glasgow, a moped rider crashes into a fence and needs treatment for a suspected broken leg. In the countryside, a fisherman has had a stroke in a remote location and needs to be rescued by the Special Operations Team using their special offroad vehicle.

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: The outbreak of smallpox in Trefelin is finally brought under control, but Prem is not convinced they have located the actual source of the disease. Rev Todd struggles to maintain his authority and locks away his daughter from prying eyes, while Megan makes a difficult decision. Comedy drama, starring Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their culinary journey through time as they celebrate British food. There is nothing like a hearty breakfast to start the day, but this vital morning meal has continued to change and evolve through time

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to the Scottish Borders with a couple who want their £450,000 budget to buy them an equestrian property in the country. Jules also visits the dramatic ruins of Melrose Abbey, where the heart of Scotland's medieval king Robert the Bruce is believed to be buried

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The return of the makeover show, with Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich designing gardens for a man in Southampton, who has a £5,000 budget for a party space to entertain his friends, but which must also have a feeling of his Welsh homeland about it

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Ninety-three-year-old D-Day veteran Thomas Hassell brings along a treasured item with a proud history - a smart military swagger stick awarded to him while on army service in Berlin for consistently being the smartest soldier on parade. The stick is a cherished symbol of a long and proud career, but sadly the decades have not been kind, and the silver head is badly dented and discoloured. It's down to silversmith Brenton West to knock out the dents and give the stick its swagger back. The team also restores a miniature whisky barrel and an antique set of scales

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: Party Political Broadcast by the Brexit Party

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Harry Judd present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Hidden Wales with Will Millard
programme.desc: The writer and adventurer gains exclusive access to explore the usually inaccessible locations that are home to some of the biggest engineering projects in Wales, both past and present. He kayaks over the highest navigable aqueduct in the world, visits the vital repair work to an underground Victorian engineering marvel, witnesses the defueling of Wales' last nuclear reactor and discovers how one of the biggest freight planes the world has ever seen is constructed

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker, Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by former footballer Dion Dublin, three-time BMX world champion Shanaze Reade, Olympic athlete Iwan Thomas and England rugby union winger Marland Yarde

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Whitney is shaken from yesterday's courtroom debacle but Sonia reassures her to put it all behind her, especially as Mick is testifying today. Karen is broken from the funeral, so Mitch makes her an offer. Kat is desperate for money to pay Suki's increased rent - and when Kush comes up trumps, Jean cannot help but be suspicious

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and presenter Adil Ray hosts the satirical quiz, inviting comedian and writer Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Conservative MP Nicky Morgan to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian and Never Mind the Buzzcocks host returns to learn another four trades, beginning with one of the most dangerous jobs in Britain - that of a deep-sea fisherman. It's a tough life, and before long he faces seasickness, fish that need gutting and a very irritated skipper. And on top of all that, he has to get up early. In the battle of fish versus funnyman, there can only be one winner

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host reviews trending news stories before chatting to guests, both in the studio and remotely. Strictl head judge Shirley Ballas discusses her autobiography Behind the Sequins, Hollywood star Ewan McGregor discusses being back on the road for his latest travelogue Long Way Up, dancer and presenter Ashley Banjo promotes the new Diversity tour Connected, while music superstar Miley Cyrus chats and performs her current single Midnight Sky. Plus, members of a reduced-size audience try to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce takes on the challenge of giving up sex for a month, visiting a psychologist to understand her own desires and investigating the pressures young people experience in their sex lives owing to online porn. She meets a celibate rapper who intends to wait until his wedding night and gets some tips on finding alternative ways to connect with her partner

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Nora takes a job at a real estate office and becomes a hyper-competent assistant with a little help from Adderall, which alienates her online gaming friends in the process. Comedy, starring Awkwafina

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Nora attempts to cash a cheque to pick her car up from an impound lot. However, her problems mount as she discovers that her bank has closed her account. Comedy, starring Awkwafina

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at the celebrity specials, which saw famous faces swapping showbusiness for big business to raise more than £2million for charity. Highlights include Karren Brady's debut on the show, heading up the women's team in the first celebrity special back in 2007, Jack Dee and Jonathan Ross attempting to design toys, and the moment Omid Djalili lost a carefully placed balloon - not to mention his dignity - as part of Rylan Clark-Neal's fundraising cabaret show

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Jason Atherton and special guest Vic Reeves. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain focuses on something close to her heart - baking delicious treats to share with loved ones. Nadiya comes from a large family, so baking for others has always taken centre stage in her own home. She kicks things off with her chilli and cranberry brioche wreath, featuring fluffy indulgent dough balls surrounding an entire baked camembert cheese, creating the ultimate tear and share. Over the years, Nadiya has become an expert in catering for big family gatherings, and she has learnt that there is nothing better for impressing guests than a gigantic stack of delicious desserts. Her mini pecan pies filled with clotted cream ensure that everyone has their own little parcel of perfection. Next up is a cheat's bread-and-butter pudding, using ice-cream and shop-bought croissants to slash the traditional cooking time without compromising on flavour

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's Nations League fixtures, including England v Belgium and Republic of Ireland v Wales

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up with a development he first saw in Anthorn, Cumbria in 2012, and the team follows projects in Hounslow, west London, and Crewe in Cheshire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, where experts Mark Stacey and Raj Bisram help competing teams bag the best buys to make a profit at auction

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps couple looking for a home in Ceredigion on a budget of £355,000, and also heads to the small town of Llandysul to meet a dynamic dairy farming family

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms three items from the Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey, including a collection of fishing rods, and a reproduction designer chair

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers compete to design an Ibiza-themed party garden for a family in Wokingham, Berkshire. All concerned hope to come up with some cool ideas to transform this unloved plot into a laid-back Balearic chill out lounge on a budget of £3,000

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway climbs the Nash Point lighthouse, a place he used to visit as a child. His coastal walk takes him along the 'Jurassic' Glamorgan coastline. On the way he goes fossil hunting, searches for an elusive chough and learns the story of a pregnancy-testing frog. He begins his walk at St Donat's Castle, once owned by newspaper magnate Sir Randolph Hearst, which became a party palace for the rich and famous, then heads for Dunraven Bay

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special family edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants Marcus Brigstocke, Helen Brigstocke, David Seaman, Frankie Seaman, Naughty Boy, Aisha Khan, Toby Tarrant and Fia Tarrant take part

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are mother and daughter Shirley and Siobhan from London, radio DJs and best friends Mike and Brian from Glasgow, and engaged couple Rachel and Raj from Hampshire

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: Little Mix band members Perrie, Jesy, Leigh-Anne and Jade search for a female dance group, with the all-singing, all-dancing hopefuls auditioning individually before being tested alongside each other, with only best making the final line up and going through to the performance shows

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: In the final episode, Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman, the judges and the show's professional dancers look back at some of the most memorable moments from Strictly Come Dancing finals, reminiscing about some of the most emotional and nerve-wracking nights inside the Strictly ballroom. This special episode also pays tribute to the 2014 Strictly champion, Caroline Flack

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the second episode, Jack and Michaela, a young couple from Wigan, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Captain America: The Winter Soldier (2014)
programme.desc: The superhuman soldier becomes an operative for secret intelligence agency SHIELD, but grows suspicious of their motives. Following a battle with a mysterious cyborg assassin, he and fellow Avenger Black Widow become fugitives, and try to bring down a sinister conspiracy. Comic-book thriller sequel, starring Chris Evans, Scarlett Johansson, Samuel L Jackson and Robert Redford

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game between Chicago Bears and Tampa Bay Buccaneers. Plus, a look at the major news stories shaping the American football world, as the leading sides in the NFL continue their bids to succeed Kansas City Chiefs as Super Bowl-winners

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: The outbreak of smallpox in Trefelin is finally brought under control, but Prem is not convinced they have located the actual source of the disease. Rev Todd struggles to maintain his authority and locks away his daughter from prying eyes, while Megan makes a difficult decision. Comedy drama, starring Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their culinary journey through time as they celebrate British food. There is nothing like a hearty breakfast to start the day, but this vital morning meal has continued to change and evolve through time

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to the Scottish Borders with a couple who want their £450,000 budget to buy them an equestrian property in the country. Jules also visits the dramatic ruins of Melrose Abbey, where the heart of Scotland's medieval king Robert the Bruce is believed to be buried

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The return of the makeover show, with Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich designing gardens for a man in Southampton, who has a £5,000 budget for a party space to entertain his friends, but which must also have a feeling of his Welsh homeland about it

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Ninety-three-year-old D-Day veteran Thomas Hassell brings along a treasured item with a proud history - a smart military swagger stick awarded to him while on army service in Berlin for consistently being the smartest soldier on parade. The stick is a cherished symbol of a long and proud career, but sadly the decades have not been kind, and the silver head is badly dented and discoloured. It's down to silversmith Brenton West to knock out the dents and give the stick its swagger back. The team also restores a miniature whisky barrel and an antique set of scales

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Scottish Green Party

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Harry Judd present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond walks in the footsteps of the old cattle drovers and finds out about the annual cattle sale in Crieff, the centre of the Scottish cattle trade. Anne Lundon investigates how the huge influx of campervans this year is impacting Scotland's roads and Euan McIlwraith explores how beef labelling will be affected by leaving the EU, and Arlene Stuart meets a couple who distil their own rum

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker, Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by former footballer Dion Dublin, three-time BMX world champion Shanaze Reade, Olympic athlete Iwan Thomas and England rugby union winger Marland Yarde

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Whitney is shaken from yesterday's courtroom debacle but Sonia reassures her to put it all behind her, especially as Mick is testifying today. Karen is broken from the funeral, so Mitch makes her an offer. Kat is desperate for money to pay Suki's increased rent - and when Kush comes up trumps, Jean cannot help but be suspicious

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and presenter Adil Ray hosts the satirical quiz, inviting comedian and writer Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Conservative MP Nicky Morgan to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes needs someone to look after Grandad so she can treat herself to a much-needed holiday - but with private nursing homes being so expensive, it seems her only option is to convince the health visitor he has lost his marbles. Rory and Dino move in for a couple of weeks, and the matriarch is soon spitting feathers when the latter begins taking over the house, and her late husband gets in touch from beyond the grave with some ominous news. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host reviews trending news stories before chatting to guests, both in the studio and remotely. Strictl head judge Shirley Ballas discusses her autobiography Behind the Sequins, Hollywood star Ewan McGregor discusses being back on the road for his latest travelogue Long Way Up, dancer and presenter Ashley Banjo promotes the new Diversity tour Connected, while music superstar Miley Cyrus chats and performs her current single Midnight Sky. Plus, members of a reduced-size audience try to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Nora takes a job at a real estate office and becomes a hyper-competent assistant with a little help from Adderall, which alienates her online gaming friends in the process. Comedy, starring Awkwafina

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Nora attempts to cash a cheque to pick her car up from an impound lot. However, her problems mount as she discovers that her bank has closed her account. Comedy, starring Awkwafina

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at the celebrity specials, which saw famous faces swapping showbusiness for big business to raise more than £2million for charity. Highlights include Karren Brady's debut on the show, heading up the women's team in the first celebrity special back in 2007, Jack Dee and Jonathan Ross attempting to design toys, and the moment Omid Djalili lost a carefully placed balloon - not to mention his dignity - as part of Rylan Clark-Neal's fundraising cabaret show

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Jason Atherton and special guest Vic Reeves. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain focuses on something close to her heart - baking delicious treats to share with loved ones. Nadiya comes from a large family, so baking for others has always taken centre stage in her own home. She kicks things off with her chilli and cranberry brioche wreath, featuring fluffy indulgent dough balls surrounding an entire baked camembert cheese, creating the ultimate tear and share. Over the years, Nadiya has become an expert in catering for big family gatherings, and she has learnt that there is nothing better for impressing guests than a gigantic stack of delicious desserts. Her mini pecan pies filled with clotted cream ensure that everyone has their own little parcel of perfection. Next up is a cheat's bread-and-butter pudding, using ice-cream and shop-bought croissants to slash the traditional cooking time without compromising on flavour

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's Nations League fixtures, including England v Belgium and Republic of Ireland v Wales

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up with a development he first saw in Anthorn, Cumbria in 2012, and the team follows projects in Hounslow, west London, and Crewe in Cheshire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, where experts Mark Stacey and Raj Bisram help competing teams bag the best buys to make a profit at auction

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps couple looking for a home in Ceredigion on a budget of £355,000, and also heads to the small town of Llandysul to meet a dynamic dairy farming family

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms three items from the Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey, including a collection of fishing rods, and a reproduction designer chair

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie and the Rich brother's face one of their hardest ever garden rescues to date in Stowmarket, turning a concrete space into a family friendly back garden

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special family edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants Marcus Brigstocke, Helen Brigstocke, David Seaman, Frankie Seaman, Naughty Boy, Aisha Khan, Toby Tarrant and Fia Tarrant take part

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are mother and daughter Shirley and Siobhan from London, radio DJs and best friends Mike and Brian from Glasgow, and engaged couple Rachel and Raj from Hampshire

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: Little Mix band members Perrie, Jesy, Leigh-Anne and Jade search for a female dance group, with the all-singing, all-dancing hopefuls auditioning individually before being tested alongside each other, with only best making the final line up and going through to the performance shows

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: In the final episode, Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman, the judges and the show's professional dancers look back at some of the most memorable moments from Strictly Come Dancing finals, reminiscing about some of the most emotional and nerve-wracking nights inside the Strictly ballroom. This special episode also pays tribute to the 2014 Strictly champion, Caroline Flack

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the second episode, Jack and Michaela, a young couple from Wigan, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Captain America: The Winter Soldier (2014)
programme.desc: The superhuman soldier becomes an operative for secret intelligence agency SHIELD, but grows suspicious of their motives. Following a battle with a mysterious cyborg assassin, he and fellow Avenger Black Widow become fugitives, and try to bring down a sinister conspiracy. Comic-book thriller sequel, starring Chris Evans, Scarlett Johansson, Samuel L Jackson and Robert Redford

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game between Chicago Bears and Tampa Bay Buccaneers. Plus, a look at the major news stories shaping the American football world, as the leading sides in the NFL continue their bids to succeed Kansas City Chiefs as Super Bowl-winners

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Louise Minchin and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela meets fed-up pedestrians who want to bring an end to motorists parking on the pavement. They say it is dangerous and inconsiderate - drivers say they have no choice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Lucy Alexander follow the progress of properties in the London borough of Camden, Coatbridge in North Lanarkshire and Bootle on Merseyside

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A former policeman in Northern Ireland forced to rebuild his life after a rocket attack on his vehicle and a Leeds paramedic threatened by a hospital patient with a 12-inch knife

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: An accident on the Isle of Mull leaves a man seriously injured, triggering a response from the air ambulance. A tourist in Edinburgh has breathing problems

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Raj Bisram help two teams scour an antiques fair in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, while Natasha Raskin Sharp sets a challenge for some other people attending

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: The quarantine continues and the supplies start getting low, and Prem is no wiser about the source of the virus. Megan and Emlyn come up with a plan to get more food from the next village, but events take a turn when Mary arrives with terrible news

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the British tradition of cooking with food hunted from the land and prepare a selection of dishes using rabbit, grouse and venison

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple looking for a property in Gloucestershire on a budget of £850,000 and visits an ice cream-making family on Rodburgh Common

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Oxfordshire mum Sarah is after a space where she can relax and unwind after a full-on day minding two young children, but is also aware that the garden also needs to work as a play space for the youngsters. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Bicester to assist Sarah and partner Adam, who need a garden the whole family can play and relax in

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team tackle a shattered ceramic dish, a hand-made doll's house and a vintage harmonium that provided the soundtrack to many an East End knees-up

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The day of Chantelle's funeral arrives, and as family and friends gather to say goodbye, Gray struggles to hold it together. Whitney is about to face her first day in court for the death of Leo, with Sonia there for support - but neither of them are prepared for what happens next. Phil visits Raymond in hospital, feeling an instant bond with the boy, while Jean warns Kush about the risks of online gambling

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: A busy Friday sees almost 700 party-mad people checking in. But with just minutes to go, there are problems with the plumbing. It's even worse for Charley, who has arrived for a hen party organised by her brother, as there are problems with their room - and general manager Damian struggles to placate them. Meanwhile, owners Laurence and Katie throw an extravagant circus-themed party for the twins' fourth birthday - and all the staff are invited

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at the celebrity specials, which saw famous faces swapping showbusiness for big business to raise more than £2million for charity. Highlights include Karren Brady's debut on the show, heading up the women's team in the first celebrity special back in 2007, Jack Dee and Jonathan Ross attempting to design toys, and the moment Omid Djalili lost a carefully placed balloon - not to mention his dignity - as part of Rylan Clark-Neal's fundraising cabaret show

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests

programme.title: Football Highlights
programme.desc: Bosnia and Herzegovina v Northern Ireland. Stephen Watson presents highlights of the Euro 2021 qualifying play-off from the Stadion Grbavica in Sarajevo. Victory for either side would see them host Slovakia or Republic of Ireland for a place in the Euro 2020 finals. With analysis by Jim Magilton

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week. On the panel are Gillian Keegan MP, Minister for Apprenticeships and Skills, Conservative; Andy Burnham, Mayor of Greater Manchester and former Health Secretary, Labour; Dame Donna Kinnair, General Secretary and Chief Executive of the Royal College of Nursing; Yanis Varoufakis, economist, author and former Finance Minister of Greece; and Michael Portillo, television presenter and former Conservative MP and Defence Secretary

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present the weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Cycling legend Chris Hoy, Winter Olympic skeleton gold medallist Amy Williams, former England cricketer Matthew Hoggard and Great Britain 400m runner Perri Shakes-Drayton join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: How one of the biggest online shopping companies in the world has left its customers wishing they had taken their business elsewhere

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Wednesfield, Wolverhampton, Sydenham in south London and Chatham in Kent, then talk to the new owners

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A spate of burglaries at Christmas leads to a police community support officer and his colleague being attacked, as they track down and try to arrest the thief. A West Midlands police officer returns to work after an assault, only to be attacked again just weeks later. Plus, a West Yorkshire police officer starts his retirement needing physiotherapy after being attacked

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Glasgow, the Special Operations Team races to locate a man who has got into difficulty while swimming in the Clyde, and another who has gone missing in water. Also in Glasgow, a moped rider crashes into a fence and needs treatment for a suspected broken leg. In the countryside, a fisherman has had a stroke in a remote location and needs to be rescued by the Special Operations Team using their special offroad vehicle.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles looks back at some of the most eclectic toys and games bought by the teams over the years, and offers advice on how to spot a bargain. Plus, Charlie Ross learns about an early board games manufacturer from Gloucester

programme.title: Lorgan Linda (Linda's Story)
programme.desc: John and Lorna Norgrove discuss the 2010 death of their daughter Linda. The 37-year-old was working for the American aid agency DAI International when she was kidnapped by Afghan rebels. For two weeks her captors led her into a hostile mountain region, moving between a series of remote farmsteads. On October 8, Linda was killed by an American grenade during a pre-dawn rescue, which also saw all of her captors die. This programme traces the emotional journey taken by family and friends in the years since, and their determination not to apportion blame, which has earned them international respect

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Dave, Amanda, Pin and Eve pit their wits against quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but if anyone is caught they're out for good, leaving fewer players for the final chase

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Luke finds Jamie unconscious on the floor at Home Farm, Billy guesses Dawn has a secret, and Charity feels lonely because she has not heard from Vanessa

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v Wales (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch introduces coverage of the friendly encounter at Wembley Stadium, as the sides meet for the first time since 2016. Form is hard to gauge at this stage of the season, especially given how the fixture list has been affected during 2020, but Gareth Southgate will be looking for an improvement from his side, who failed to impress in their recent fixtures against Iceland and Denmark. Ryan Giggs, in contrast, will have taken great heart from Wales' performances against Finland and Bulgaria. With analysis by Ian Wright, Roy Keane, Chris Coleman and Lee Dixon, and commentary by Sam Matterface

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: England v Wales. Action from the friendly at Wembley Stadium, as the rivals battled it out for bragging rights. The sides last met in the group stage of Euro 2016, when Gareth Bale gave the Welsh the lead, but the Three Lions prevailed 2-1 thanks to goals by substitutes Jamie Vardy and Daniel Sturridge

programme.title: Out of Their Skin
programme.desc: Part two of two. Ian Wright charts the inspiring rise of black footballers in Britain through archive footage, original interviews and a compilation of individual stories from the past and present. Contributors include Ashley Cole, Raheem Sterling, John Barnes, Paul Ince, Rhian Brewster and Gareth Southgate

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott re-creates his favourite takeaway dishes - Indian, Chinese and Italian. Guests Anita Rani and Jeremy Pang also help the chef prepare dishes

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef arrives in the riverside town of L'Isle-sur-la-Sorgue in south-eastern France, where his culinary hero Keith Floyd once owned a restaurant. Buying produce from its floating market, and a Floyd-style hat, James sets up his mobile kitchen on a bridge overlooking the river, and cooks peaches pain perdu in the sunshine. Heading back in the direction of the town, James samples some truffle-laden dishes at renowned restaurant La Beaugraviere

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage from Newmarket, including the Dewhurst Stakes and the Cesarewitch. Plus, three races from York. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Adele Mulrennan, reports by Oli Bell, Matt Chapman and Luke Harvey, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Competitors from across the nation test their strength, speed and courage in this obstacle-course challenge, hosted by Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara. Those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show, with former footballer-turned-TV-presenter Dion Dublin, Countdown's Rachel Riley and comedian Tim Vine all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Susan Calman, Dermot Murnaghan, Perri Shakes-Drayton and Omid Djalili pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: The 14th series of the talent search comes to a close with the spectacular grand final. The remaining acts - including comedians Steve Royle and Jon Courtenay, dancing duo Aaron and Jasmine, and magician Magical Bones - go head to head to win the hearts and votes of the viewing public, with the winner landing a £250,000 prize and a slot at this year's Royal Variety Performance. Presented by Ant and Dec, with judges Amanda Holden, David Walliams, Alesha Dixon and Ashley Banjo

programme.title: Jonathan Ross' Comedy Club
programme.desc: Jonathan and his co-host comedian Mawaan Rizwan welcome some of the UK's best new stand-ups to the comedy club, including the hilarious Aurie Styla, Fern Brady and Babatunde Aléshé. Plus, special guest Russell Kane will be trying out some new material

programme.title: Raging Bull (1980)
programme.desc: Biopic of boxer Jake LaMotta, who rose to fame as the world middleweight champion in the 1940s. His career was marred by an inability to deal with his anger, leaving his family bearing the brunt of his rage and paranoia. Directed by Martin Scorsese and starring an Oscar-winning Robert De Niro alongside Joe Pesci and Cathy Moriarty

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty hosts a quiz show in which 16 families take part in a knock out tournament as they attempt to win the holiday of a lifetime and the title of Britain's Brightest Family. programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v Wales (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch introduces coverage of the friendly encounter at Wembley Stadium, as the sides meet for the first time since 2016. Form is hard to gauge at this stage of the season, especially given how the fixture list has been affected during 2020, but Gareth Southgate will be looking for an improvement from his side, who failed to impress in their recent fixtures against Iceland and Denmark. Ryan Giggs, in contrast, will have taken great heart from Wales' performances against Finland and Bulgaria. With analysis by Ian Wright, Roy Keane, Chris Coleman and Lee Dixon, and commentary by Sam Matterface

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: International Football Highlights
programme.desc: England v Wales. Action from the friendly at Wembley Stadium, as the rivals battled it out for bragging rights. The sides last met in the group stage of Euro 2016, when Gareth Bale gave the Welsh the lead, but the Three Lions prevailed 2-1 thanks to goals by substitutes Jamie Vardy and Daniel Sturridge

programme.title: Out of Their Skin
programme.desc: Part two of two. Ian Wright charts the inspiring rise of black footballers in Britain through archive footage, original interviews and a compilation of individual stories from the past and present. Contributors include Ashley Cole, Raheem Sterling, John Barnes, Paul Ince, Rhian Brewster and Gareth Southgate

programme.desc: In Manchester, Josh, Scott and Tim go up against each other in a series of shopping challenges hoping to win the affections of Sarah from Lancashire

Harry Hill narrates

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: In the second part of the last-ever episode, the question of whether hard-living raconteur Charlie Harper is really dead is finally answered, as Alan and his friends are terrorised by a vengeful figure with a penchant for sending ominous notes. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer, with a few surprise cameos to mark the long-running show's final goodbye

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan and Jake become one-and-a-half men as they bid a tearful goodbye to Charlie, but their numbers are soon bolstered when they meet internet billionaire Walden Schmidt. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan gets amnesia after falling off an arcade game during a CIA operation and Francine sees it as the perfect opportunity to mould him into her ideal husband. Meanwhile, Steve is drawn into the corrupt side of Roger's paper route business.

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: Latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Yogi Bear (2010)
programme.desc: A conniving bear and his diminutive sidekick learn that the national park they live in is due to be closed down by the greedy local mayor. They must join forces with their old foe, a park ranger who resents their mischief-making, to save the day. Comedy based on the cartoon series, starring Anna Faris and Tom Cavanagh, and featuring the voices of Dan Aykroyd and Justin Timberlake

programme.title: Looney Tunes: Back in Action (2003)
programme.desc: Daffy Duck tires of playing second fiddle to Bugs Bunny, so sets off to seek his fortune. He ends up helping a would-be stuntman rescue his screen idol father, in reality a secret agent, who has been kidnapped by the deranged chairman of the Acme Corporation. Live action and animated comedy, with Brendan Fraser, Steve Martin, Timothy Dalton and Jenna Elfman

programme.desc: Latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: The Nut Job 2: Nutty By Nature (2017)

programme.title: The Nut Job 2: Nutty By Nature (2017)

programme.title: King Kong (2005)
programme.desc: Maverick film-maker Carl Denham and his cast and crew board a ship bound for a mysterious island - but when they arrive, a giant gorilla carries off their leading lady, Ann Darrow. As the crew makes its way through the teeming jungle to find her, the director concocts a dangerous scheme to capture the great ape and take it back to New York. Peter Jackson's remake of the fantasy adventure, starring Naomi Watts, Jack Black, Adrien Brody and Andy Serkis, in a dual role as the ship's cook and motion-captured creature

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: Latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Celebrity Karaoke Club
programme.desc: More famous faces battle it out on stage in the hope of becoming the ultimate karaoke star. This week's edition features the arrival of former X Factor star Diana Vickers

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Lois receives a surprising birthday present from her father, an unfortunate accident results in Carter needing to live under the Griffins' care

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire must figure out how to live without his most valuable body part. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian search for Mort Goldman after learning there is a $10,000 reward for turning him into the police

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Francine wishes to relive her kids' childhoods with a family trip to a theme park, but disaster strikes when the cryonically-frozen founder of the attraction is mistakenly thawed out and takes them prisoner in the Medieval area

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: In the 150th episode of the animated comedy, Jeff is stranded on Roger's home planet, and must prove to the evil Emperor Zing that he was always in love with Hayley. However, no one has yet succeeded in passing such a test

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Lester appears to take his own life after losing his savings when he invests in Cleveland's beer truck business. Meanwhile, tired of having his hair cut by his mother, Rallo opens a barbershop in the kitchen

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna becomes jealous when a slimmer, prettier version of Cleveland's former school friend 'Fatty Patty' turns up and reveals she has a crush on him. However, the newcomer goes crazy and tries to steal Cleveland's 'essence', forcing Donna to intervene

programme.title: Release the Hounds
programme.desc: Geordie Shore stars Marnie Simpson, Chloe Ferry and Nathan Henry swap the Toon for a spooky forest in eastern Europe. Horror game show, hosted by Reggie Yates

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Footage of someone sets a record for holding a tarantula in their mouth, as well as bubble blowing, marathon hugging and inserting needles into a person's head

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally is annoyed when Kevin refuses to help with the children. Alec tries to get Vera to agree to hosting an Irish Night. Gerry tries to present Liz with a gift but she refuses it

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre urges Liz to tell the police about the jewellery. Rita blames Derek for ruining her relationship with Fred. Curly decides to sell up and leave Weatherfield

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Steve lies unconscious in the road and Sophie and Zoe rush to his aid. The police arrive to talk to Kim about the incident. Rachel spends the night with Jack

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel starts to feel more comfortable about her affair with Jack, and Ned discovers evidence of the fling. Terry and Zak both worry about their lack of success with women

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police investigate a hit-and-run incident in Ashfordly and discover evidence suggesting it may have been an act of revenge - with the victim's own mother-in-law one of the possible suspects. Meanwhile, Peggy finds herself in trouble again when her latest batch of illegal cigarettes turn out to contain an unexpected ingredient

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin borrow Mr Roper's car to drive to Southampton for a football match. However, the vehicle has similar qualities to its unreliable owner and soon breaks down leaving the duo having to summon help from an unexpected quarter. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin regrets sampling Mr Roper's home brew when he is breathalysed during a driving lesson with Chrissy. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: A valuable heirloom brought back from the China Sea could change the Ropers' fortunes - but not if Mildred's sister gets it first. Starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Newly promoted DS Hazel Wallace returns from training to investigate when a man is found hanged - a case further complicated by the fact that, according to police records, the victim actually died a year previously. It is not long before Jack is back from his extended compassionate leave to help her sort out the whole sorry mess. David Jason stars, with Caroline Harker, Kate McEnery and Cheryl Campbell

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Gina is shocked to discover a severed hand in a dustbin and the subsequent police investigation unearths evidence which suggests someone on Lord Ashfordly's estate may be responsible. Meanwhile, Peggy invests in a freezer for her latest business venture, but soon finds her new purchase at the centre of the murder case. Last in series, starring Tricia Penrose, Gwen Taylor and Jonathan Kerrigan

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak is upset about Lisa and Albert's engagement. Albert confides in Marlon that Lisa has got it wrong and he did not propose to her. Alex wants to continue his affair with Kim

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel warns Jack that Ned knows about their affair. Zak tells Albert he wants him to leave when he marries Lisa. Alex tells Kim that he loves her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Judy is upset that Joyce has gone back to her thieving ways. Liz changes the locks to her flat. Ken is glad when Deirdre agrees to stay the night with him

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz feels isolated and thinks is being controlled by a maniac. Sally is horrified to find Mike has sacked Tricia. Anne tells Eric that Curly is planning to leave

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of a childless farmer's wife puts Barnaby on the scent of an ancient land feud over Setwale Wood. Potions, witchcraft, marital infidelity and a ruthless campaign to save the area form the heady brew that convinces the detective he is dealing with a case of murder. Wendy Craig guest stars, with John Nettles and Daniel Casey

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The severed arm of a missing environmental health inspector is washed up on the shores of the Humber and Frost's investigation leads him to a local meat factory. Unfortunately, his new lodger's annoying dog keeps distracting him from the case. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Emma Kay, Niall O'Brien and Tom Georgeson

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When psychologist Reuben Beatty is murdered while moonlighting as a psychic, Lewis and Hathaway struggle to unravel the victim's baffling double life. Dark forces appear to be at work, and not just in the afterlife. Sanjeev Bhaskar guest stars, with Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox, Clare Holman and Rebecca Front

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. With Geoff now a marked man, he and daughter Evie are put under watch at a hotel while the search for Mullen is widened. However, when the suspect follows Evie's boyfriend to the location and sets off the fire alarm, he draws Geoff and Evie outside. Meanwhile, Banks believes loan shark Hargreaves is behind the hit on Marcus, and manages to extend his stay in the cell, despite having very little evidence against him. The conclusion of the two-part case, starring Stephen Tompkinson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The severed arm of a missing environmental health inspector is washed up on the shores of the Humber and Frost's investigation leads him to a local meat factory. Unfortunately, his new lodger's annoying dog keeps distracting him from the case. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Emma Kay, Niall O'Brien and Tom Georgeson

programme.desc: The trio move out to allow workmen into the flat, and Robin is more than happy to share a bed - until he finds out who with. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Mildred tries to make an impression at the Young Conservatives' ball, whereas George is determined to find his own Christmas spirit at the Red Lion

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The severed arm of a missing environmental health inspector is washed up on the shores of the Humber and Frost's investigation leads him to a local meat factory. Unfortunately, his new lodger's annoying dog keeps distracting him from the case. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Emma Kay, Niall O'Brien and Tom Georgeson

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An infamous London gangster arrives in the area to try to rebuild his life after prison, but Sergeant Miller has other ideas. Meanwhile, Peg starts a salmonella outbreak in Aidensfield when her miracle chicken feed scam goes awry

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of two meetings. From Newmarket, races start at 1.50, 2.25, 3.00, 3.35 and 4.10. From York, at 2.45 and 3.55. With commentary by Richard Hoiles, analysis from Jason Weaver and Hayley Turner, and reports by Matt Chapman and Rishi Persad

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned wants to know Jack's intentions when Sarah returns. Steve is in a rush to set a wedding date. Linda, Zoe, Kelly, Kathy and Sophie perform as the Spice Girls for Ned's birthday

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack turns on Ned for meddling in his affairs. Rachel is reluctant to talk to Sarah at work. Lisa is upset that she is stuck marrying Albert when it is Zak she wants

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tricia is stunned to hear Sally resigned over her sacking. Des is furious with Derek for spoiling Becky's party. Liz cancels visiting Steve as she does not want to be near Fraser

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Grizzled copper Jack Frost teams up with an obnoxious, chain-smoking colleague to investigate a seemingly straightforward hit-and-run incident that proves to have devastating repercussions for the victim's family and their peaceful rural community. David Jason, Bruce Alexander and Philip Jackson star

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa worries Dr Timoney's suggestion that she and Martin aren't meant to be together might have prompted him to leave Portwenn for good. Calling on Ruth, Morwenna and Janice for help, she tries to track the down the missing GP, with no luck. However, what Dr Ellingham's friends and family fail to realise is that he is actually trapped up at the Winton farm, where desperate wife Annie is trying to coerce him into performing a life-saving operation on her terminally ill husband Jim. Meanwhile, Ruth and Bert seek out Al to offer him a proposition regarding the whisky and the B&B. Medical drama, starring Martin Clunes

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Portwenn is abuzz with activity for the preparation of Janice and Penhale's wedding, but a medical issue could prevent Penhale from making it down the aisle. Meanwhile, Al is struggling with the demands of being best man, especially when Bert's marketing antics mean there is only whisky available at the pre-wedding party. Martin Clunes and John Marquez star

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of student Josh Tate is found dumped in a ravine, the investigation into his stabbing uncovers a very different man to the quiet character described by his flatmate Spencer. It emerges that Josh was infatuated with Spencer's girlfriend and had been manufacturing Ecstasy for club owner Liam Fallon. Meanwhile, DI Morton's ex-lover Martin Hexton has arrived, claiming to be working on a local case. When Fallon comes under suspicion as Josh's murderer the detectives break into his flat and find him badly beaten - and to complicate matters, it looks as if Hexton is responsible

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. Morton works hard to win back Banks's approval and discovers Hexton is the biological father of murdered student Josh. The team traces the former undercover police officer to Gerald and Elaine Foster's house, but it's too late - Hexton has kidnapped lap-dancer Melanie. When his victim collapses, however, he takes her to hospital, where ex-lover Helen corners him and has him arrested. What will he reveal about Josh and Melanie, and the attack on club owner Liam? Stephen Tompkinson stars

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Grizzled copper Jack Frost teams up with an obnoxious, chain-smoking colleague to investigate a seemingly straightforward hit-and-run incident that proves to have devastating repercussions for the victim's family and their peaceful rural community. David Jason, Bruce Alexander and Philip Jackson star

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin's hopes for a night of romance are dashed when Chrissy decides to supervise his girlfriend's visit. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox

programme.title: Carry On Jack (1963)
programme.desc: A bumbling midshipman's uniform is stolen by a woman desperate to go to sea, who adopts his identity. Later, the hapless sailor is press-ganged on to the same ship as her. However, while trying to prove who he really is, he has to contend with mutinies, pirates and the Spanish Navy. Comedy, with Bernard Cribbins, Juliet Mills, Kenneth Williams and Charles Hawtrey

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Steve and Leanne join forces to give Oliver a fighting chance, Billy confides his worries about Paul to David, and Sally and Tim plan a secret wedding

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When a spate of petty thefts culminates in a series of murders at a student hostel, Miss Lemon persuades the Belgian sleuth to investigate. As ever, he receives support from Chief Inspector Japp. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Philip Jackson, Pauline Moran, Damian Lewis and Paris Jefferson

programme.title: Carry On Cleo (1964)
programme.desc: A captured Briton is mistaken for a heroic swordsman and promoted to the Roman Royal Guard, with orders to protect Julius Caesar. But the last thing he and the fated emperor expect is an assassination attempt from within Caesar's own circle of friends. Comedy, starring Amanda Barrie, Kenneth Williams, Sid James, Kenneth Connor, Jim Dale, Charles Hawtrey and Joan Sims

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Barnaby launches a murder investigation when the severed hand of a missing hotel manager is found by a dog. The dead man had recently been named in the will of the hotel owner, and solving the case takes on a renewed sense of urgency when fatal accidents start happening to the other beneficiaries. Mystery, guest starring Rosemary Leach and Samantha Bond, with John Nettles and Daniel Casey

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A horse whisperer disappears in Newton Magna, bringing Barnaby face to face with long-standing adversary Melvyn Stockard. The former convict's social climbing has paid dividends, sending him straight to the top of the village pecking order - and some believe his driving ambition makes him capable of foul play. Murder mystery, guest starring Larry Lamb (EastEnders, Gavin & Stacey), with John Nettles and Daniel Casey

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prominent resident is robbed and battered to death with her walking stick, and suspicion soon falls on the members of her reading club - which turns out to be a front for a lucrative investment portfolio. John Nettles and Daniel Casey star, with guest appearances by Anton Rodgers (Fresh Fields) and Angela Thorne (To the Manor Born)

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: Marc Warren steps into the shoes of Barry Foster to take on the role of Dutch detective Van der Valk in this updated adaptation of the Amsterdam-based crime drama

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: The crime drama starring Stephen Tompkinson returns with the first of a two-part story. When the detective suffers a personal loss, he's forced to navigate a complex murder case while grieving. A young Estonian woman has been found dead, with the evidence suggesting she was buried alive. Piecing together her movements, the team uncovers a dark world of prostitution and drugs, and investigates her connection to a single father, his autistic son, and a family who seem to have silenced the town

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The officers are dismayed to learn the arson attack on the Osgoods' house has injured Michael and killed his son Robbie, and local bully Gary McCready is soon questioned. As the investigation into Katrin's murder continues, the body of an older man who died of natural causes is found buried near her grave, and when Banks suspects Jason McCready abducted missing Annika, he uncovers a shocking secret. Meanwhile, the detective gives a moving speech at his mother's funeral, and the ceremony brings him closer to his father

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of eminent lawyer Anaan Kamel is found washed up by an underground river, suspicion falls on Marcus Layton, a former employee of the victim's chambers who was recently fired by her husband Raheel. Text messages suggest an affair that had progressed to blackmail, but Banks and Morton struggle to find further information about Marcus, as his records only seem to date back five years. And when his corpse is found upstream in what looks to be a contract killing, the case becomes much more complicated
