programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned wants to know Jack's intentions when Sarah returns. Steve is in a rush to set a wedding date. Linda, Zoe, Kelly, Kathy and Sophie perform as the Spice Girls for Ned's birthday

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack turns on Ned for meddling in his affairs. Rachel is reluctant to talk to Sarah at work. Lisa is upset that she is stuck marrying Albert when it is Zak she wants

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tricia is stunned to hear Sally resigned over her sacking. Des is furious with Derek for spoiling Becky's party. Liz cancels visiting Steve as she does not want to be near Fraser

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Grizzled copper Jack Frost teams up with an obnoxious, chain-smoking colleague to investigate a seemingly straightforward hit-and-run incident that proves to have devastating repercussions for the victim's family and their peaceful rural community. David Jason, Bruce Alexander and Philip Jackson star

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa worries Dr Timoney's suggestion that she and Martin aren't meant to be together might have prompted him to leave Portwenn for good. Calling on Ruth, Morwenna and Janice for help, she tries to track the down the missing GP, with no luck. However, what Dr Ellingham's friends and family fail to realise is that he is actually trapped up at the Winton farm, where desperate wife Annie is trying to coerce him into performing a life-saving operation on her terminally ill husband Jim. Meanwhile, Ruth and Bert seek out Al to offer him a proposition regarding the whisky and the B&B. Medical drama, starring Martin Clunes

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Portwenn is abuzz with activity for the preparation of Janice and Penhale's wedding, but a medical issue could prevent Penhale from making it down the aisle. Meanwhile, Al is struggling with the demands of being best man, especially when Bert's marketing antics mean there is only whisky available at the pre-wedding party. Martin Clunes and John Marquez star

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of student Josh Tate is found dumped in a ravine, the investigation into his stabbing uncovers a very different man to the quiet character described by his flatmate Spencer. It emerges that Josh was infatuated with Spencer's girlfriend and had been manufacturing Ecstasy for club owner Liam Fallon. Meanwhile, DI Morton's ex-lover Martin Hexton has arrived, claiming to be working on a local case. When Fallon comes under suspicion as Josh's murderer the detectives break into his flat and find him badly beaten - and to complicate matters, it looks as if Hexton is responsible

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. Morton works hard to win back Banks's approval and discovers Hexton is the biological father of murdered student Josh. The team traces the former undercover police officer to Gerald and Elaine Foster's house, but it's too late - Hexton has kidnapped lap-dancer Melanie. When his victim collapses, however, he takes her to hospital, where ex-lover Helen corners him and has him arrested. What will he reveal about Josh and Melanie, and the attack on club owner Liam? Stephen Tompkinson stars

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Grizzled copper Jack Frost teams up with an obnoxious, chain-smoking colleague to investigate a seemingly straightforward hit-and-run incident that proves to have devastating repercussions for the victim's family and their peaceful rural community. David Jason, Bruce Alexander and Philip Jackson star

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin's hopes for a night of romance are dashed when Chrissy decides to supervise his girlfriend's visit. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox

programme.title: Carry On Jack (1963)
programme.desc: A bumbling midshipman's uniform is stolen by a woman desperate to go to sea, who adopts his identity. Later, the hapless sailor is press-ganged on to the same ship as her. However, while trying to prove who he really is, he has to contend with mutinies, pirates and the Spanish Navy. Comedy, with Bernard Cribbins, Juliet Mills, Kenneth Williams and Charles Hawtrey

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Steve and Leanne join forces to give Oliver a fighting chance, Billy confides his worries about Paul to David, and Sally and Tim plan a secret wedding

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When a spate of petty thefts culminates in a series of murders at a student hostel, Miss Lemon persuades the Belgian sleuth to investigate. As ever, he receives support from Chief Inspector Japp. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Philip Jackson, Pauline Moran, Damian Lewis and Paris Jefferson

programme.title: Carry On Cleo (1964)
programme.desc: A captured Briton is mistaken for a heroic swordsman and promoted to the Roman Royal Guard, with orders to protect Julius Caesar. But the last thing he and the fated emperor expect is an assassination attempt from within Caesar's own circle of friends. Comedy, starring Amanda Barrie, Kenneth Williams, Sid James, Kenneth Connor, Jim Dale, Charles Hawtrey and Joan Sims

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Barnaby launches a murder investigation when the severed hand of a missing hotel manager is found by a dog. The dead man had recently been named in the will of the hotel owner, and solving the case takes on a renewed sense of urgency when fatal accidents start happening to the other beneficiaries. Mystery, guest starring Rosemary Leach and Samantha Bond, with John Nettles and Daniel Casey

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A horse whisperer disappears in Newton Magna, bringing Barnaby face to face with long-standing adversary Melvyn Stockard. The former convict's social climbing has paid dividends, sending him straight to the top of the village pecking order - and some believe his driving ambition makes him capable of foul play. Murder mystery, guest starring Larry Lamb (EastEnders, Gavin & Stacey), with John Nettles and Daniel Casey

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prominent resident is robbed and battered to death with her walking stick, and suspicion soon falls on the members of her reading club - which turns out to be a front for a lucrative investment portfolio. John Nettles and Daniel Casey star, with guest appearances by Anton Rodgers (Fresh Fields) and Angela Thorne (To the Manor Born)

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: Marc Warren steps into the shoes of Barry Foster to take on the role of Dutch detective Van der Valk in this updated adaptation of the Amsterdam-based crime drama

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: The crime drama starring Stephen Tompkinson returns with the first of a two-part story. When the detective suffers a personal loss, he's forced to navigate a complex murder case while grieving. A young Estonian woman has been found dead, with the evidence suggesting she was buried alive. Piecing together her movements, the team uncovers a dark world of prostitution and drugs, and investigates her connection to a single father, his autistic son, and a family who seem to have silenced the town

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The officers are dismayed to learn the arson attack on the Osgoods' house has injured Michael and killed his son Robbie, and local bully Gary McCready is soon questioned. As the investigation into Katrin's murder continues, the body of an older man who died of natural causes is found buried near her grave, and when Banks suspects Jason McCready abducted missing Annika, he uncovers a shocking secret. Meanwhile, the detective gives a moving speech at his mother's funeral, and the ceremony brings him closer to his father

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of eminent lawyer Anaan Kamel is found washed up by an underground river, suspicion falls on Marcus Layton, a former employee of the victim's chambers who was recently fired by her husband Raheel. Text messages suggest an affair that had progressed to blackmail, but Banks and Morton struggle to find further information about Marcus, as his records only seem to date back five years. And when his corpse is found upstream in what looks to be a contract killing, the case becomes much more complicated

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: Contessa Caroline di Contini journeys to Venice to discover what happened to George. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn and Nyree Dawn Porter

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Counterfeiters lead Simon a merry dance from London to Switzerland via Paris, and the involvement of a bogus countess only complicates matters further. Roger Moore stars, with Kate O'Mara and Philip Madoc

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The daughter of a prominent professor is terrified by a mysterious prowler who haunts the grounds of her home by night and seems able to scale sheer walls with ease. Guest starring Charles Kay, Sarah Woodward, Adrian Lukis and Alice Morphy

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed recuperates in a private hospital after breaking his leg, unaware an old enemy whom he sent to prison is plotting to murder him. Patrick Macnee and Linda Thorson star, with Ray Brooks

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur and Ray's dreams of riches Down Under fall apart. Left penniless, Ray is forced to bar work while Arthur encounters roguish street trader Derek Collins. George Cole and Gary Webster star

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: An old adversary of Doyle's stirs up rivalry between factions of political terrorist group the Organization as a diversion in order to launch a revenge attack on the CI5 operative, who he blames for the death of his brother. Action drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan uses a criminal's abandoned dog as bait to lure his remorseful owner back into the country from France. However, associates of the villain spirit the hound away from its pound and a race begins as to who can locate the dog first. Alfred Marks, Brian Glover and Brian Wilde guest star, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: Harry and the Contessa help a female journalist who is investigating a politician suspected of murder. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: John Inverdale presents coverage of the women's singles semi-finals at Roland-Garros, the final Grand Slam tournament of the delayed 2020 tennis season. British hope Johanna Konta's hopes were ended at this stage last year when she was beaten 7-5, 7-5 by Marketa Vondrousova and Ashleigh Barty came from a set down to defeat Amanda Anisimova. Match analysis comes from two-time former champion Jim Courier, former Wimbledon champion Marion Bartoli, British Fed Cup captain Anne Keothavong, Andy Murray's former coach Mark Petchey, Fabrice Santoro - who played in 70 Grand Slams - and former British number one Sam Smith. Commentary is from Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, with Celina Hinchcliffe reporting

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five old school friends answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: An orientation day gives the hapless Mr Bean a chance to revisit his school days, and it is not long before he lives up to his disaster-prone reputation. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson slips into silly mode once more as his comic alter ego visits the launderette and somehow manages to lose his trousers, getting himself into a real pickle as he tries to retrieve them. Fully dressed again, he moves on to the crazy golf course, where the relatively simple task of hitting the ball leads to utter chaos

programme.title: An Audience with Billy Connolly
programme.desc: The comedian descends on London to entertain a celebrity crowd including Twiggy, Ringo Starr, Michael Brandon, Samantha Fox and Clive James, covering topics including ageing, parties and his idea for a new national anthem

programme.title: Face/Off (1997)
programme.desc: FBI agent Sean Archer captures terrorist Castor Troy, the man who killed his son. While Troy is in a coma, Archer has the crook's face surgically grafted onto his own so he can go undercover and locate a bomb planted somewhere in Los Angeles. But then Troy regains consciousness - and in turn takes on Archer's identity. John Woo's action thriller, starring Nicolas Cage, John Travolta, Joan Allen, Gina Gershon and Dominique Swain

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: The latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Face/Off (1997)
programme.desc: FBI agent Sean Archer captures terrorist Castor Troy, the man who killed his son. While Troy is in a coma, Archer has the crook's face surgically grafted onto his own so he can go undercover and locate a bomb planted somewhere in Los Angeles. But then Troy regains consciousness - and in turn takes on Archer's identity. John Woo's action thriller, starring Nicolas Cage, John Travolta, Joan Allen, Gina Gershon and Dominique Swain

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur and Ray's dreams of riches Down Under fall apart. Left penniless, Ray is forced to bar work while Arthur encounters roguish street trader Derek Collins. George Cole and Gary Webster star

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Riggs and Murtaugh investigate a violent case featuring a drug cartel, theft, two killings and, regrettably, Murtaugh's one-time training officer, Ned Brower. As matters unfold, it transpires that Ned is even more involved than first thought, which leaves Murtaugh with a dangerous decision to make. Looking for back-up, the duo turn to Cruz for help. In the midst of all the chaos, Riggs continues to depend on outside vices rather than face the reality of Miranda's death, while Murtaugh and Trish struggle to find time to spend together. Clayne Crawford and Damon Wayans star

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A huge scandal threatens Bohemia, as Holmes is outwitted by a bewitching woman's blackmail who owns a compromising photograph of a European royal. Period detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett as the brilliant Baker Street sleuth, with David Burke as his trusty companion Dr Watson, guest starring Gayle Hunnicutt

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: A home for retired clowns is not all smiles and cream pies as Steed and Tara King look into the killing of the Directors of the Capitol Land and Development Company. John Cleese and Bernard Cribbins guest star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur pawns his wife's jewellery to solve a temporary cash-flow problem _ but soon finds himself in deeper trouble than ever. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole as shady London entrepreneur Arthur Daley

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle spring into action when East German agents blackmail a government employee into revealing secret plans for a new type of jet-engine tank. Featuring an appearance by playwright Lynda La Plante, alongside stars Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan persuades a villain to act as an informant providing evidence against his two accomplices. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman, and featuring an early screen appearance by Christopher Ellison

programme.title: World Cup Rivalries: England v Argentina
programme.desc: Short sports film about this compelling sporting rivalry.

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: John Inverdale presents coverage of the men's semi-finals at Roland-Garros, the final tennis Grand Slam of 2020. Rafael Nadal and Dominic Thiem won their last-four matches in contrasting fashions last year, with Nadal comfortably overcoming Roger Federer 6-3, 6-4, 6-2, and Thiem eventually defeating Novak Djokovic in a five-set epic. Match analysis comes from two-time former champion Jim Courier, former Wimbledon champion Marion Bartoli, British Fed Cup captain Anne Keothavong, Andy Murray's former coach Mark Petchey, Fabrice Santoro - who played in 70 Grand Slams - and former British number one Sam Smith. Commentary is from Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, with Celina Hinchcliffe reporting

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston narrates the documentary featuring another selection of real-life incidents captured by people on their personal cameras. Highlights include a man who nearly gets taken out by an unsecured load on a van, road rage incidents and a motorist taking impromptu lessons in aquaplaning after a terrifying incident on a wet motorway

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of AEW, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: Car Crash Global: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Featuring dash-cam footage of accidents from France and Russia, the top five examples of road karma from around the world and the most extreme taxi clips

programme.desc: Arthur pawns his wife's jewellery to solve a temporary cash-flow problem _ but soon finds himself in deeper trouble than ever. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole as shady London entrepreneur Arthur Daley

programme.title: Isle of Man TT
programme.desc: A look back at the most memorable supersport races in the last 10 years of the road-racing event, a 600cc discipline that often produces closely fought battles

programme.title: Isle of Man TT
programme.desc: A look back at the best stagings over the last 10 years of the meeting's blue riband event, the Senior TT

programme.title: World Superbike Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the seventh round of the season south of Paris, as the riders competed for glory at the Circuit de Nevers Magny-Cours in France. Valuable points were up for grabs, but who snatched glory at the 4.411km track in the Pirelli French Round?

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Oli Bell and the team are at Newmarket, where the Dewhurst Stakes is the feature race. Plus, a look back at last week's racing in France, headlined by the Arc

programme.title: ITV Sport Stories
programme.desc: Highlights from the ITV Sport archive

programme.title: World Cup Top Goalscorers
programme.desc: Some of the best strikers in the history of the football World Cup

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes and Dr Watson protect the last remaining member of the aristocratic Baskervilles, who believes his family is being haunted by a monstrous spectral hound roaming the remote Devon moors. Feature-length mystery based on Conan Doyle's classic story, starring Jeremy Brett, Edward Hardwicke, Ronald Pickup and Kristoffer Tabori

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the women's singles final on day 14 at Roland-Garros in Paris. Ashleigh Barty claimed her maiden Grand Slam title here last year, overcoming the unseeded Marketa Vondrousova 6-1, 6-3 in the showpiece match to become the first Australian French Open champion male or female since Margaret Court prevailed in 1973. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Marion Bartoli, Anne Keothavong, Mark Petchey, Sam Smith and Fabrice Santoro, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: ITV Sport Stories
programme.desc: Highlights from the ITV Sport archive

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi is joined by Tom Dove and Neil Spooner at the renowned Gigantica Lakes complex in the Champagne region of France, hoping to hook a huge fish

programme.title: Rambo: First Blood Part II (1985)
programme.desc: Incarcerated Special Forces veteran John Rambo is offered the chance for freedom by former commander Colonel Trautman if he takes up the offer to return to the jungles of Vietnam on a life-or-death mission to rescue American PoWs. During the course of the operation, he develops strong feelings for his guide, a female freedom fighter - but tragedy strikes, leaving him more determined than ever to exact a bloody and explosive revenge. Action adventure sequel, starring Sylvester Stallone, Richard Crenna, Julia Nickson-Soul, Steven Berkoff, George Kee Cheung, Andy Wood and Martin Kove

programme.title: In the Line of Fire (1993)
programme.desc: An ageing secret service agent, tormented by his failure to save the life of John F Kennedy in 1963, is drawn into a battle of wits with a sinister CIA-trained assassin out to kill the current president. As the day of reckoning draws closer, the agent's nerves are tested to the limit - and nothing seems to be going his way. Wolfgang Petersen's thriller, starring Clint Eastwood, John Malkovich, Rene Russo and Dylan McDermott

programme.desc: Regan persuades a villain to act as an informant providing evidence against his two accomplices. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman, and featuring an early screen appearance by Christopher Ellison

programme.title: World Cup Rivalries: England v Argentina
programme.desc: Short sports film about this compelling sporting rivalry.

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: John Inverdale presents coverage of the men's semi-finals at Roland-Garros, the final tennis Grand Slam of 2020. Rafael Nadal and Dominic Thiem won their last-four matches in contrasting fashions last year, with Nadal comfortably overcoming Roger Federer 6-3, 6-4, 6-2, and Thiem eventually defeating Novak Djokovic in a five-set epic. Match analysis comes from two-time former champion Jim Courier, former Wimbledon champion Marion Bartoli, British Fed Cup captain Anne Keothavong, Andy Murray's former coach Mark Petchey, Fabrice Santoro - who played in 70 Grand Slams - and former British number one Sam Smith. Commentary is from Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, with Celina Hinchcliffe reporting

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston narrates the documentary featuring another selection of real-life incidents captured by people on their personal cameras. Highlights include a man who nearly gets taken out by an unsecured load on a van, road rage incidents and a motorist taking impromptu lessons in aquaplaning after a terrifying incident on a wet motorway

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of AEW, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: Car Crash Global: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Featuring dash-cam footage of accidents from France and Russia, the top five examples of road karma from around the world and the most extreme taxi clips

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur pawns his wife's jewellery to solve a temporary cash-flow problem _ but soon finds himself in deeper trouble than ever. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole as shady London entrepreneur Arthur Daley

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes and Dr Watson protect the last remaining member of the aristocratic Baskervilles, who believes his family is being haunted by a monstrous spectral hound roaming the remote Devon moors. Feature-length mystery based on Conan Doyle's classic story, starring Jeremy Brett, Edward Hardwicke, Ronald Pickup and Kristoffer Tabori

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the women's singles final on day 14 at Roland-Garros in Paris. Ashleigh Barty claimed her maiden Grand Slam title here last year, overcoming the unseeded Marketa Vondrousova 6-1, 6-3 in the showpiece match to become the first Australian French Open champion male or female since Margaret Court prevailed in 1973. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Marion Bartoli, Anne Keothavong, Mark Petchey, Sam Smith and Fabrice Santoro, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: Rambo: First Blood Part II (1985)
programme.desc: Incarcerated Special Forces veteran John Rambo is offered the chance for freedom by former commander Colonel Trautman if he takes up the offer to return to the jungles of Vietnam on a life-or-death mission to rescue American PoWs. During the course of the operation, he develops strong feelings for his guide, a female freedom fighter - but tragedy strikes, leaving him more determined than ever to exact a bloody and explosive revenge. Action adventure sequel, starring Sylvester Stallone, Richard Crenna, Julia Nickson-Soul, Steven Berkoff, George Kee Cheung, Andy Wood and Martin Kove

programme.title: Countdown

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jane and Martin want a holiday home in the coastal town of Portimão in the Algarve - but Ben Hillman needs to find properties suitable for the couple and their beloved dog Bailey

programme.title: The Great House Giveaway
programme.desc: Electrician Sam and estate agent Eva meet just hours before buying a property together in Rugby, then have six months to renovate the house and sell it on for a profit. Hosted by Simon O'Brien

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Bournemouth, Dorset, Karen and Lindsay kick-off the competition with an evening of trusted family favourites, winning over garrulous David and Elaine as well as newlyweds Matt and Jay. Day two sees hopeless romantics Matt and Jay serving up a traditional Russian feast, but eyebrows are raised when shop-bought items are thrown into mix, while Matt's boy band days provokes huge intrigue from their guests. Finally, it's David and Elaine's chance to show the younger members of the competition how to throw a dinner party, providing they survive their incessant bickering in the kitchen

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer befriends holidaying Hollywood stars and promises to keep their whereabouts a secret - but cannot resist telling the gang at Moe's about his new celebrity friends. With the guest voices of Kim Basinger, Alec Baldwin and Ron Howard

programme.title: Hollyoaks

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Truth About Your Sandwich
programme.desc: Helen Skelton investigates Britain's sandwich industry, investigating the hygiene conditions in high street chains and sandwich production plants, and how cuts to hospital budgets and food inspection budgets could be putting the public at risk. She also asks whether consumers can trust the nutritional levels advertised on the sandwiches they buy

programme.title: Council House Britain
programme.desc: Frances and Thomas move from Dublin to spend their retirement in Southwark - only to discover their tower block needs major work. Yvonne (69) desperately needs a roof over her head after losing her job and the home that came with it. As coronavirus strikes, the frontline council officers carry on work within social distance rules, while pest technician Andy begins delivering food to the vulnerable

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Singers Claire and Louise arrive at the restaurant to provide a bit of moral support to each other, making a foursome with Mathew, who works in a supermarket, and engineering manager Scott. But there's confusion over who will be dating who. Meanwhile, 22-year-old Aryeh hopes fellow student Jack will not be put off by his overwhelming personality and 60-year-old Meryl from Wirral is looking for someone to keep up with her when she goes clubbing. Will Whitchurch divorcee Andrew be up for a dance?

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Rough Guide to the Future
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud sends three celebrities around the world to take a light-hearted look at technical solutions to some of the planet's biggest issues. Jon Richardson is in America to learn about the latest advances designed to prolong life, and Phil Wang goes to China to explore how technology is enabling new ways for people to earn a living. In Japan, Alice Levine learns about the robots that are becoming as advanced as humans

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to the upmarket quarter of Hollywood to help the owner of failing health-food restaurant Sante La Brea. As well as struggling to control his disrespectful staff, the proprietor receives a surprise visit from the police at the height of dinner service

programme.title: Inside the Bomb Squad
programme.desc: Sgt David Podmore is called to investigate an explosive substance in his unit's home city of Chester, where police have found an arsenal of weapons, but are unsure of a chemical giving off smoke and have evacuated the area. The highly skilled search team uses hi-tech equipment and specialist dogs to look for weapons and explosive devices at the Birmingham hotel being used by for the Conservative Party Conference

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Bedfordshire, Tom and Naomi kick off the competition with a posh menu that fails to leave an impression on fellow contestants Victoria and Elliot, and Neil and Charlie

programme.title: The Pet Talent Agency: Barking Mad
programme.desc: Documentary following Karen Chamberlain, owner of the Barking Mad pet talent agency, which supplies everything from cute pooches to cuddly cats for films, TV and ad campaigns. Whether the casting call is for dogs, sheep, ponies, zonkeys or alpacas - no job is too big, too small or too ambitious for Karen. But after a troubling dry spell at the agency, Karen and husband Pete's dwindling savings account is keeping the company afloat, so it's make-or-break time for the couple. Will a Guinness World Record attempt for Lorraine Kelly, and a high-profile job for a potential new London mayor, help improve the agency's fortunes?

Her parents, Patricia and Terry, hand the keys of their Surrey mansion to their daughter so she can help find a potential suitor, inviting a mountain-bagging explorer, a gym owner, a fashion boss, a recruitment consultant and a former golf professional to move in alongside her in a bid to kindle romance. Plus, the trio participate in a self-authored group therapy session talking about mental health, music and systemic racism, as well as how they have managed to navigate a year marked by pandemic, pain and protests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Britain's Great Cathedrals with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: The actor recounts the history of Winchester Cathedral, the longest medieval cathedral in northern Europe, which has witnessed civil wars, religious battles and struggled with collapsing foundations. It is the burial place of novelist Jane Austen - though only four people attended her funeral and originally her plaque made no mention of her writing. Tony learns that Winchester's bishops were among the richest, most influential and most badly behaved in the country and some were wealthier and more powerful than even England's kings

programme.desc: The comedian explores the Borders region, heading to a secret woodland in Galloway Forest, where she meets some cute and ardently protected red squirrels. She then enjoys a tour of Traquair House which, at 1,000 years old, has played host to historic figures such as Bonnie Prince Charlie. Susan is in her element as the 21st Lady of Traquair reveals secret stairwells and Mary Queen of Scots' bed. To the south, in Eastriggs, Susan visits the site of the world's largest munitions factory, where thousands of female workers produced explosives during the First World War

programme.desc: Officers patrol during Black Eye Friday - one of Cumbria's most alcohol-fuelled nights of the year, and help a woman trapped inside a burning car. Meanwhile, a boy racer is made to call his mother after being caught misbehaving, and a motorway pursuit is brought to an abrupt end

programme.title: How the Victorians Built Britain
programme.desc: Michael Buerk looks at how the Victorians created what is now known as the modern home, exploring the huge rise in house-building during the period. He travels to Fakenham, Norfolk, to visit the last remaining gasworks in England, and discovers how the Victorians mastered the art of producing 'town gas' from coal. He also investigates how the kitchen was transformed with the advent of gas cookers, as more complex meals including the Sunday roast steadily became the norm across the nation

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm
programme.desc: Cameras follow hill shepherds Amanda and Clive Owen and their nine children as they endure a heatwave on their remote hill farm in the Yorkshire Dales. programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm
programme.desc: All hands are on deck to prepare sheep for the auction ring, while Amanda and Clive Owen's children are busy baking entries for the village farm show. Meanwhile, Clive waits to see if his best sheep will be crowned champion, and sell for thousands of pounds

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: Jane McDonald travels along the Danube on one of the most luxurious ships of its type, as she makes a foray into the realms of the river cruise. Beginning in Budapest, Jane heads to the Szechenyi Baths, where she indulges in a game of chess. Afterwards, a visit to the Second World War memorial Shoes on the Danube Bank, leaves her in tears. Heading onwards to Vienna, the presenter is all set to meet a real-life princess. Then, after a waltz and a wurst, Jane makes her way to her final stop of Passau, southern Bavaria, to view its charming architecture

programme.title: Agatha Christie: 100 Years of Poirot & Miss Marple

A potential new pupil, Minnie the rottweiler, arrives in Warrington, but before she can be offered a place, she needs to have her behaviour assessed. Daycare boss David calls on the assistance of his right-hand dog, Zeus the Siberian husky, to help with the assessment. Zeus can spot an anti-social dog within minutes - so will Minnie pass the test?

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Property expert Ann Maurice and designer Alistair Appleton present a compilation of tips on making a house presentable for sale, including ideas for clearing clutter and creating the illusion of space

programme.title: Dark Command (1940)
programme.desc: A teacher is defeated in his ambition to become the marshal of his town during the early years of the American Civil War, so he turns renegade instead. Before long he has a reputation as a feared raider fighting on the side of the South, and it falls to the newly appointed lawman whose job he coveted to stop his reign of terror. Western, starring John Wayne and Walter Pidgeon

programme.title: The Naked Truth (1957)
programme.desc: Celebrities threatened with blackmail decide they would rather murder the extortionist than pay him, but things do not go according to plan. Comedy, starring Terry-Thomas, Peter Sellers, Dennis Price, Peggy Mount, Shirley Eaton and Joan Sims

programme.title: 40 Guns to Apache Pass (1967)
programme.desc: A cavalry captain sets out to deliver a shipment of rifles to a fort besieged by marauding Apaches, but the mission is undermined by a villainous corporal who plans to steal the weapons and sell them to the enemy. Western, starring Audie Murphy, Michael Burns, Laraine Stephens, Kenneth Tobey and Michael Keep

programme.title: The Buccaneer (1958)
programme.desc: A general charged with the defence of New Orleans in 1812 learns the British are sending a vast fleet to retake the city. His only hope is to form an alliance with a notorious pirate - but while the buccaneer's loyalties are to his homeland, his crew urges him to side with the invaders. Remake of Cecil B DeMille's classic swashbuckling adventure, directed by Anthony Quinn, starring Yul Brynner, Charlton Heston, Claire Bloom and Charles Boyer

programme.title: Walking Out (2017)
programme.desc: An urban teenager journeys to Montana to hunt big game with his estranged father. The duo struggle to connect, until a brutal encounter in the wilderness changes everything. Adventure, starring Matt Bomer, Josh Wiggins and Bill Pullman

programme.title: Three Billboards Interview Special
programme.desc: Writer-director Martin McDonagh and star Sam Rockwell talk about making the dramatic comedy about revenge and redemption in small-town America

programme.title: Three Billboards Outside Ebbing, Missouri (2017)
programme.desc: Grieving mother Mildred Hayes has suffered an unimaginable personal loss, and hatches an idea that drags the residents of Ebbing, Missouri into a maelstrom of revenge. She pressures Sheriff Bill Willoughby and his forces into continuing the investigation into her daughter's murder. It becomes an obsession that has dramatic repercussions for all of the locals. Martin McDonagh's Bafta and Oscar-winning crime drama, starring Frances McDormand, Sam Rockwell and Woody Harrelson

programme.title: Seven Psychopaths (2012)
programme.desc: A writer struggling to complete the script for a movie about psychopathic killers turns to his criminal friends for advice. He ends up getting more than he bargained for when the small-time crooks kidnap a dog that belongs to a gangster, putting all their lives in danger. Comedy thriller, starring Colin Farrell, Sam Rockwell, Christopher Walken and Woody Harrelson

programme.title: Beauty and the Dogs (2017)
programme.desc: Mariam is subjected to a horrific night of rape and sexual abuse by police officers. She is treated with suspicion and contempt by medical staff when she reports what has happened and is met with hostility by the police. Thwarted at every turn by the institutions that should be protecting her, can Mariam find any sort of justice for what's happened to her? Tunisian crime thriller, starring Mariam Al Ferjani. In Arabic

programme.title: The Turning Point (1952)
programme.desc: A journalist helps a crusading district attorney who has been given the task of cracking down on a crime syndicate, but the pair's efforts are hampered by political corruption. Film noir, starring William Holden, Edmond O'Brien and Alexis Smith

programme.title: Anna and the King (1999)
programme.desc: A widowed schoolmistress leaves Victorian England to tutor a son of the King of Siam, only to discover his majesty actually expects her to care for all 58 of his children. In spite of disagreements and many ups and downs, the teacher gradually forms a deep platonic love for her royal employer. Period drama, starring Jodie Foster, Chow Yun-Fat, Ling Bai, Syed Alwi, Tom Felton and Randall Duk Kim

programme.title: Tora! Tora! Tora! (1970)
programme.desc: Fact-based Second World War drama recounting the bombing of Pearl Harbour from both the Japanese and American perspectives, reconstructing the events leading up to the devastating attack on the US naval base in 1941. Starring Martin Balsam, So Yamamura, Joseph Cotten, James Whitmore and Jason Robards

programme.title: Only the Brave (2017)
programme.desc: Eric Marsh, the superintendent of a group of elite firefighters, is pushed to his limit when he and his crew tackle a historic wildfire. Fact-based drama, starring Josh Brolin, Jennifer Connelly and Andie MacDowell. Directed by Joseph Kosinski, helmer of Tron: Legacy and Top Gun: Maverick

programme.title: Layer Cake (2004)
programme.desc: A London cocaine dealer is all set to leave the criminal world behind and start a new life abroad. However, his plans are thwarted when he is asked to track down a gangster's drug-addicted daughter and locate a stolen shipment of Ecstasy. Crime thriller, starring Daniel Craig, Michael Gambon, Colm Meaney, Kenneth Cranham and Sienna Miller

programme.title: Carrie (1976)
programme.desc: A shy, withdrawn teenager discovers she has paranormal powers, which she uses to protect herself from her abusive mother and classmates. When a friendly student invites her to the prom, she is determined to enjoy herself and is amazed when she is crowned queen of the ball - but joy turns to fury when her tormentors play a cruel prank. Brian De Palma's adaptation of Stephen King's horror novel, with Sissy Spacek, Piper Laurie and Amy Irving

programme.title: Stoker (2013)
programme.desc: A teenager living an isolated life in her mother's remote estate discovers she has an uncle she has never met following her father's death. The mysterious relative moves into the house and seems to take over his brother's role in the family. His niece suspects he has sinister motives, but becomes dangerously infatuated with him. Thriller, starring Mia Wasikowska, Nicole Kidman and Matthew Goode

programme.title: Those Glory, Glory Days (1983)
programme.desc: A journalist with a passion for football has a chance meeting with her soccer idol Danny Blanchflower, triggering memories of 1961, and her teenage obsession with Tottenham Hotspur as well as her close friendship with three other equally football-obsessed girls. Drama, starring Zoe Nathenson, Julia McKenzie, Sara Sugarman, Cathy Murphy and Blanchflower as himself

programme.title: Carry On Regardless (1961)
programme.desc: A businessman starts up an odd-job agency, but employs seven hopeless handymen incapable of completing any task without causing utter chaos. Matters come to a head when their landlord tries to evict them - forcing the bungling workmen to finally prove their worth. Slapstick comedy, starring Sid James, Charles Hawtrey and Kenneth Williams

programme.title: Shrek Forever After (2010)
programme.desc: The grumpy ogre longs for his simple life as a feared monster, so strikes a deal with magical trickster Rumpelstiltskin to get back to the way things were. However, he soon finds there is a catch - he ends up stuck in an alternate world where none of his friends know who he is and where Rumpelstiltskin has become the kingdom's tyrannical ruler. Animated comedy, with the voices of Mike Myers, Eddie Murphy and Cameron Diaz

programme.title: Percy Jackson: Sea of Monsters (2013)
programme.desc: The modern-day teenage son of the Greek god Poseidon must lead his friends on a perilous journey into the Bermuda triangle. They need to recover the legendary Golden Fleece to save the world, but getting to it means braving all manner of terrifying monsters. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Logan Lerman and Alexandra Daddario

programme.title: Star Trek (2009)

programme.desc: Leonard and Sheldon receive an invitation to present their research to a physics conference, but Sheldon's reluctance to pander to his peers causes conflict between the friends. An intervention by Penny makes matters worse. Johnny Galecki and Jim Parsons star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Leonard invent excuses to avoid having to attend Penny's performance in a production of the stage musical Rent, but maintaining the pretence makes matters increasingly complicated. Johnny Galecki, Simon Helberg and Kaley Cuoco star, with DJ Qualls

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon falls ill, and his friends decide to go to a Planet of the Apes movie marathon to keep their distance, knowing he will be in a terrible mood. However, an unsuspecting Penny offers to nurse him. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Simon Helberg and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: Warcraft (2016)
programme.desc: A kingdom stands on the brink of war when it is invaded by a vast horde of creatures who have travelled through a portal from their dying world. As dark magic works its influence on the world, two leaders from either side are set on a collision course with each other as they strive to protect their people. Fantasy adventure based on the best-selling computer game, starring Travis Fimmel, Paula Patton and Ben Foster

programme.title: X-Men: Days of Future Past (2014)
programme.desc: In a future ravaged by war, the superhero team makes a desperate last stand against a force of deadly robots. Their only hope is to send Wolverine back in time to the 1970s, to join forces with an earlier version of the X-Men and change the events that started the conflict. Superhero adventure sequel uniting the casts of the original movie and prequel First Class, starring Hugh Jackman, Jennifer Lawrence, James McAvoy and Michael Fassbender

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The fly-on-the-wall series turning the cameras on TV's most vocal armchair critics features their thoughts on programmes from September 2018. Shows under consideration include talent show The X Factor, espionage drama Bodyguard, documentary Reported Missing, science programme The Joy of AI and slimming series The Extreme Diet Hotel

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on TV from the first week of June, 2017, featuring The Spy Who Loved Me, Andrew Neil with Jeremy Corbyn, the SNP's manifesto, Paul Hollywood's Continental Road Trip, the Green Party and SNP's Party Election Broadcasts, Your Face or Mine, Great British Skinny Dip and the news

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Professional singer Precious seeks romance with amateur magician Earle. The restaurant also welcomes company director John, who hopes fellow Scot Greig is the man he can settle down with. And fellow diner Hebe is on the verge of giving up on finding Mr Right. Hopefully she will connect with fine wine merchant Charles

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Astronaut-in-training Nicky hopes to find romance with well-travelled motoring journalist Daniel. The restaurant also welcomes road sweeper Bruce, whose date is mother of two Yvonne. After years of being single, she's ready to put herself first and to find that one true love to keep her company into her twilight years

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Former boxing champ and groom-to-be Jordan plans a fight-night wedding in front of thousands of people, but his fiancee Danielle dreams of a girly and intimate wedding day

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake is excited to work a case with the best detective in the NYPD, Dave Majors - until his colleague shares his intentions to ask Amy out on a date. Rosa's surprising fashion choice causes a stir, and Charles and Gina make an unexpected discovery. Comedy, with Andy Samberg

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Holt begins to worry he is being set up for a fall by one of his superiors, and Peralta is handed the chance to work on an important case. Meanwhile, Jeffords tries to make sure his twins get a place at their chosen school. Comedy, with Andy Samberg and Terry Crews

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp visits Manhattan, where she creates candy canes and lollipops at artisan sugar crafter Papabubble, before heading back to the UK to learn how to make passion fruit marshmallows covered in chocolate and coconut

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Married couple Steve and Dawn first visited Mijas on the eastern coast of Spain 11 years ago and have wanted to own a holiday home there ever since. They eventually plan to live in Spain, so the pressure is on for Laura Hamilton to show them five alternate properties within their maximum budget of £220,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole makes a table out of a part from a jet in Tiverton, and Simon O'Brien finds a pair of exhaust pipes which he thinks could become a trendy pair of lamps

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Worcestershire to meet mechanic Adrian. Simon restores an old, wrecked armchair into some punk-rock furniture

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick gets to work on the walled garden in the spring and buys a mini tractor for the job - but it may not fit through the gate. Angel designs another suite, with black walls and mosaic mirrors, and decorates the entire dining room with blossoms so a guest can make a special wedding proposal. The couple also consider the possibility of truffle farming

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton heads to Tyne and Wear, to help Duncan and Susan Marshall build a very large Georgian-inspired family home. However, the Georgian look proves difficult to accomplish and Charlie has to intervene to help the pair achieve an authentic feel for their new home

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to the Old Times Guesthouse in Ipswich, where mother-and-daughter duo Linda and Kelly Langdown hope a go-karting session will get the contest off to a flying start. However, a lack of breakfast options and corner-cutting on the guests' orders do not go down very well

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests visit the Flower Pot in Sunbury-on-Thames on their second night, where owner Simon Bailey and French manager Pierre Cudon provide stylish continental-inspired accommodation, but the hosts are left puzzled by some of the feedback they receive the next morning

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to the family-run Dolphin Hotel in Exmouth, Devon, which has been a life's work for hotelier Jo Knowles. On arrival, Pat and Nigel find a suspicious stain, before the guests put on their war faces for a game of paintball. Jo takes everyone to a floating restaurant for dinner, and the next morning she presents a huge spread for breakfast - but her diners are suspicious that it has been laid on specially for the competition

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants stay at the Mariners Beachside B&B in Seaton, Devon, but are disappointed by the cleanliness of the property. They take part in a town-crying competition during the afternoon, and host Nigel feels under pressure to deliver his award-winning breakfast the next morning. Come feedback time, the owners discover not all their guests share the same opinion

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the final time to find out what they have been paid, and confront their rivals. Simon Bailey and Pierre Cudon from the Flower Pot feel the feedback left by Nigel and Pat Booth is a step too far, while the Mariners B&B owners hit back at the men's opinion of their establishment's cleanliness. Linda and Kelly Langdown of the Old Times Guest House question the rating of their facilities, while the Dolphin Hotel's Jo Knowles has a confession to make regarding her hosting

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Cameras follow Chester Zoo's four new fledgling Humboldt penguins who are being brought up apart from the main colony until they're old enough to compete for fish in the main pool. They're keen to make a break for it and join the adults, but when they're finally free to go they have other ideas. Elsewhere, Niall the chimpanzee keeper has known chimp Boris for 44 years. When the ape captures a moorhen chick, Niall must use his powers of persuasion to negotiate the safe release of his hostage

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer return to find out how some of the families they have helped are getting on since they decided whether to redesign their problematic homes or sell up and move on. They begin with Paul and Dawn from the first edition back in April 2015, who were at loggerheads over what to do with their detached country home. Its isolated location on the outskirts of Huddersfield made it perfect for astronomy fanatic Paul, but Dawn was desperate to move, hating its dysfunctional layout

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel look ahead to wedding season, with eight couples booking the chateau for their big day. Dick also converts a stone barn into a flat for Angel's parents, getting rid of 150 years' worth of rubbish in the process, but tensions rise between the couple when he gets carried away with the trees at the front of the property

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits a project undertaken by Daren Howarth and Adi Nortje, a couple who moved from Brighton to Brittany to create an earth-sheltered house built almost entirely from recycled materials. Their aim was to enlist the help of unskilled volunteers to work on the home, in exchange for teaching them construction techniques to keep down the costs. The presenter returns to learn how their experiment turned out

programme.title: A Year in the New Forest
programme.desc: A look at life in the New Forest in the spring, a challenging time of year for both people and animals. The semi-wild cattle and ponies that live in the forest face giving birth during changeable weather conditions, while the possibility of a late frost puts a goshawk's eggs at risk

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow Lawrence, 26, who is rushed to St George's after being hit by a car while crossing the road on a night out in Eastbourne. Meanwhile, 53-year-old Katrina is brought to A&E after having difficulty breathing and experiencing chest pains that could indicate a heart attack

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words and numbers quiz, with Vic Reeves and Sara Pascoe joining team captains Sean Lock and Lee Mack. Alex Horne and the Horne Section join Countdown regular Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley provides the numbers and letters

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The first episode of the hit comedy. The Craggy Island cleric dreams of achieving stardom on the Faith of Our Fathers TV show - but his hopes are dashed by Jack and Dougal's untimely intervention. Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon and Frank Kelly star

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: A sense of dread descends on Craggy Island as the priests prepare for poker-faced Father Stone's annual visit - and Ted tries desperately to think of a scheme to get rid of him. Clerical comedy, starring Dermot Morgan

programme.title: The Accident
programme.desc: Sarah Lancashire stars in the drama set in a fictional Welsh town where an explosion at a construction site that has killed several teenagers. In the aftermath of the tragedy, Polly worries that husband Iwan might be hiding something. Angela gathers the families to fight for justice, and as Harriet faces questions at work, her assistant - and lover - Tim acts to protect her by leaking information that drives a wedge through the town. Co-starring Mark Lewis Jones, Joanna Scanlan and Sidse Babett Knudsen

programme.title: Shocking Emergency Calls
programme.desc: First-hand accounts of deadly wildfires in Paradise, California, that wiped out homes, towns and lives, telling the stories of the emergency responders and those who escaped

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman uses all of his property know-how to find the dream home in Barbados for Julie and her husband John. After years of holidaying in the Caribbean, they can't wait to buy a property there, where they can spend their winters soaking up the glorious sunshine. With a budget of £300,000, will any of the five properties on offer impress them enough to make an offer?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel to Surrey to meet upcycler Rob, who has a range of sheds a few miles from Gatwick Airport

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Wokingham in Berkshire to meet professional gardener Mark whose barns are brimful of bikes, bric-a-brac and baggage

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel look ahead to wedding season, with eight couples booking the chateau for their big day. Dick also converts a stone barn into a flat for Angel's parents, getting rid of 150 years' worth of rubbish in the process, but tensions rise between the couple when he gets carried away with the trees at the front of the property

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton helps Cheshire architect Matthew Lewis, who is trying to build the perfect home for a very demanding client - Hayley, his wife. Hayley, a GP, has found an extraordinary rural site on the edge of the Peak District, and Matthew plans to use his own building firm to create a contemporary single-storey family home that makes the most of the stunning views. To make ends meet, the couple have been living in a caravan and the pressure is on Matthew to finally put a proper roof over the family's head

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to the Borgue Hotel in Dumfries and Galloway, which owners Vince and Shirley Barrett run with military precision. On arrival, guests Adam and Naomi are not impressed with the decor, while Ken and Elaine do not like the orange towels. Later, over dinner, the hosts reveal who wears the trousers in their relationship, before Ken's jokes go down like a lead balloon

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Allscott Mill B&B in Telford, Shropshire, on their second night, where hosts Ken and Elaine Purchase welcome their guests. On arrival, Shirely and Vince find fault with the lack of headroom in their lodgings, while Adam and Naomi are bemused by the absence of a toilet brush in their bathroom

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to Park House in Llandrindod Wells, Powys, where owners Ian and Joan Barr hope their colourful B&B will impress their guests. Adam and Naomi are wowed by the vibrant decor, but Ken and Elaine take issue with a dirty toilet brush

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Naomi and Adam Black welcome the contestants to East Thorne yurt retreat in Bude, Cornwall. They are pleased to discover it is Vince and Shirley's dream location, but Ken and Elaine don't share the same opinion. Later, the guests take part in an archery competition, and in the morning, are unimpressed with the DIY breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rival owners meet for the final time on the last day of the competition to find out what they've been paid and settle some scores. Hosting skills, breakfasts, facilities and two toilet brush gripes are aired before the cash is counted and the winner is announced

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: With numbers in the wild dwindling rapidly, Chester Zoo's black rhino breeding programme is a key part of preserving the species. Black rhino Kitani is introduced to new mate Magadi, but with injury a high risk during mating, keeper Babs is concerned that Kitani may struggle to defend herself, since she lost her horn after the death of her calf. Meanwhile, there's a surprise birth in the orangutan house, and older sibling Tuti has to fend for herself and set out on the road to independence

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet Keeley and Terence in Woodford in London who are unsure whether they should renovate their current home or sell up and move into another. Keeley feels that their current family home is no longer suitable for their family of five and longs for a larger property, but Terence loves the location of their house and thinks that renovating it would be a better option

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As summer arrives, Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree are busier than ever with seven weddings and 14 food lovers' weekends to prepare for. As if that wasn't enough to keep them busy, they must also sort out the garden, work on a stables conversion and embark on a surprising floating adventure

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits carpenter Bill Bradley and his wife Sarah, who set out to build two timber houses on a very limited space on the site of an old wood workshop in south London. The presenter finds out whether their dream of a mortgage-free life came true despite the complications posed by the awkward location, and a soaring budget

programme.title: 23 Cases
programme.desc: Rapp has revoked his confession to another murder - that of the beautiful Helena Hussek - and now Dr Christian Bohn is implicated. His wife, Barbel, has provided his alibi, but more information leads the team to look deeper into the marriage. Meanwhile, Henry's mistrust of Rapp comes to a head. Thriller, starring Shadi Hedayati and Franz Dinda. In German

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow a 10-year-old who has severed his finger at school and a stonemason who has dropped a slab of marble on his hand. Meanwhile, a 74-year-old partially sighted woman is brought in by her neighbour after breaking her ankle in a fall, and draws the medical staff into stories of her challenging childhood

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Mechanic Grant, 34, is rushed in after being crushed against a brick wall by his own car, which he was working on at the time. Doctors are concerned he has a serious pelvic injury and other internal wounds and work quickly to establish how severe they are. Meanwhile, 44-year-old Doug, who has a rare growth on his head and face which is prone to profuse bleeding, arrives in A&E, and 23-year-old cage fighter Charlie is treated in minor injuries for something in his eye

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Denver, Colorado, where he meets the stubborn owner of Italian restaurant Pantaleone's, who appears to be firmly stuck in the past. Pete maintains he makes the best pizza in town - based on a review from the 1980s - but the fiery chef's own taste test proves the opposite. With Pete's wife Paulette and son Josh threatening to leave, can Gordon get through to him and save the business?

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: A man suffers multiple injuries after a quad-bike accident on a farm near Penrith. Reported anaphylaxis following a bee sting has the heli-medics racing to the aid of Melody. Plus, cyclist Vivienne is in a bad way after her bike leaves the road on the notoriously steep Wrynose Pass in Cumbria

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: House-hunters Charlotte and Ben are looking to move their young family lock, stock and barrel to the Central American country of Costa Rica. With their £200,000 budget, Ben Hillman shows them five family homes. However, it's a huge life-changing decision, so will any of them be the right fit for the adventurous pair?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Richard has dreamed of owning a holiday for 20 years and thinks Nosara on Costa Rica would be the perfect location, so Danni Menzies lines up five options for his £275,000 budget

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer catches up with two couples who struggled to find their first marital home, including Rupreet and Sundeep who had their hearts set on a specific area in Harrow. Phil also revisits Paul and Elly, who searched for a property in Bristol, to see if they found the perfect house

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets a couple who have built their own houses in the past, but have now come up with a scheme far more ambitious than anything they have undertaken previously. David and Greta Iredale's new home is being designed and fabricated in Germany, then transported to Surrey to be assembled within a week. Once it has been erected, they face the challenge of putting their own stamp on the property

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Eco campaigner Hector Christie holds the first dinner party in Devon, hoping his selection of raw food and home-grown produce will win him the £1,000 cash prize. His guests - make-up artist Kirsten Brend, slate salesman Damion Fearnley, theatre nurse Jenny Fox and retired housekeeper Viv Foster - are more interested in the stately home he lives in, so the host reveals an unusual surprise to command their attention

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Make-up artist Kirsten Brend showcases her Danish roots while hosting the second dinner party in Devon, serving a traditional menu to celebrate her heritage and some Eurovision-style entertainment to demonstrate her singing skills

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Slate salesman Damion Fearnley hosts the third dinner party in Devon, hoping to impress his guests with a carefully crafted menu of restaurant quality food. No dish is as simple as it sounds, and Damion has given each one his own little twist. Unfortunately for him, two of the diners seem more intent on taking verbal swipes at each other than bites out of his food

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Theatre nurse Jenny Fox's dinner party in Devon fails to get off to the best start when Damion Fearnley is not impressed by the dishes on offer and Hector Christie takes exception to her choice of main course. Meanwhile, the tension between Viv Foster and Kirsten Brend continues as they show no sign of putting their disagreement behind them

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Retired housekeeper Viv Foster hosts the final night in Devon, and feels in confident mood after years of experience serving the rich and famous. She pulls out all the stops to impress with a butler, silver service and her favourite tried and tested recipes. With tension mounting between Viv and Kirsten for some time though, her plans to put on the perfect evening may yet be sabotaged

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the Five Bells near Dover, where manager Shelley Parnell and her competitive mum Linda Yates hope their bargain-priced B&B will win the day

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The hosts check in to the Thomas Paine Hotel in Thetford, where an inappropriate book is found in a bedside drawer and competition turns fierce at a Home Guard training exercise

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Chris and Sarah Whitehead invite their fellow hoteliers to Tenbury Wells in Worcestershire, where they open the doors of their historic pub The Rose and Crown

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Lesley and Steve Hawker welcome the contestants to the Woodlands Guest House in Exmoor, but after the bad feedback of previous rounds, it seems their rivals are out for revenge

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet up to find out what they have been paid. Shelley and Linda from The Five Bells want to know why their feedback for breakfast doesn't add up, while Gez from the Thomas Paine Hotel wants to find out why Shelley and Linda scored him so low. Chris and Sarah from The Rose and Crown want to question Gez, and Steve and Lesley from Woodlands Guest House try to get to the naked truth

programme.title: Emergency Rescue: Air, Land & Sea
programme.desc: Humberside Search and Rescue rush to Thornwick Bay to help an injured man, and the Cairngorm Mountain Rescue team embarks on a night search to find a missing biker

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A five-year-old is rushed into St George's struggling to breathe, and her mum reveals how she has spent months in Great Ormond Street Hospital after being diagnosed with a rare form of juvenile arthritis. Meanwhile, an 83-year-old man in a confused state arrives in A&E after falling over, and as doctors carry out tests and a CT scan, his wife helps them understand the reason behind his confusion. Plus, a twelve-year-old girl must be brave as she undergoes treatment for a nasty cut on her leg

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: In an edition of the documentary focusing on fathers and sons, 51-year-old builder Rod is rushed into A&E after falling 18 feet from scaffolding onto concrete. When scans reveal that he has fractured some of his vertebrae and will require extensive surgery, his wife Nicky is left to call their two sons to prepare them for bad news. Meanwhile, 86-year-old retired policeman John is brought to St George's with a head injury after falling off a wall. As doctors assess the severity of the damage, he talks about how his career took over his life, so much so that he missed the birth of his son. Plus, 19-year-old cyclist Mack requires specialist help for a dislocated shoulder, after he collided with a Porsche. His dad explains how adopting him and his brother changed all of their lives

programme.title: Father Ted

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson spares no expense when he tests the BMW X6. James May joins Margaret Calvert, one of Britain's road-sign designers, to test-drive the Vauxhall Insignia VXR, and Richard Hammond tries out a £340,000 Lexus supercar. Plus, musician Seasick Steve is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads north towards the Arctic Circle to explore the culinary delights of Reykjavik, coastal capital of Iceland. His aim is to enjoy the perfect winter break, enriched with a smorgasbord featuring the best ingredients the country has to offer - fjord-reared roast lamb, rye bread baked in volcanic sand and langoustine soup. Rick also resumes his search for the perfect cod

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Berlin, where history and the avant garde dwell side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall and Checkpoint Charlie now teems with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs offering delicious seasonal creations, such as pureed sunflower seeds, Jerusalem artichoke flans and crushed frozen pine nuts. Loved by the likes of Greta Garbo, Charlie Chaplin and Auguste Escoffier there's still a cornucopia of choice for those with a more traditional temperament with eisbein, meatballs and sausages topping every menu in the German capital

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston travels to Sardinia to meet the scientists and archaeologists who have evidence that suggests giants once existed. Researchers have discovered abnormally large human skeletons in networks of elaborate caves, mines and tombs in Sardinia, China, the United States, northern India and South America. However, if the race did exist, it remains unclear as to why it has seemingly been eradicated from history

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring ship graveyards, including Scapa Flow in Orkney, where German warships were scuttled after the First World War, and Mallows Bay, home to 200 wrecks colonised by wildlife

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston travels to Jerusalem in search of Solomon's Temple. When the Romans sacked the city in 70AD, the fabled Treasure of Solomons was believed to have been looted and taken back to Rome. However, it is possible it was hidden away by the High Priests. Archaeologists Dr Shimon Gibson and Kate Raphael have dedicated their lives working out what actually happened, and discuss whether it is possible that Solomonic and Roman treasures lie undiscovered under Jerusalem

Plus, Paul McCartney's solo performance of Lady Madonna

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave gets a nasty surprise when he opens the bonnet of a fast Ford from the 80's, and an Austin Heavy 12 nearly a century old sets off a bidding war at the auction

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek, with help from the Mathewson clan, drag a mini pickup out of the barn where it's been for 20 years, and a 350cc Douglas Mark V motorbike from 1950 goes under the hammer

programme.title: Steam Train Britain
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of three preserved steam railway lines - the South Devon Railway heritage line, the Ffestiniog and Welsh Highlands Railway and the Great Central Railway. Sports scientist Megan Smith has a dream of tending the fire of a steam locomotive on the Ffestiniog & Welsh Highland Railway, and she stokes the bolier of NG143 and sets off along the 25 miles from Caernarfon to Porthmadog

programme.title: Ganges
programme.desc: Exploring the story of the river, following its course from the Himalayas to their foothills. The programme reveals its enormous influence on the day-to-day living of a continent, how it has shaped Indian culture and religion and why it attracts a vast array of wildlife including snow leopards, Asiatic brown bears and bearded vultures

programme.title: Ganges
programme.desc: How the Gangetic Plains, once a wilderness teeming with wildlife, have been transformed into one of the most densely populated places on Earth - and how the region's tigers, lions, cheetahs and other species have been affected by human encroachment on their habitat

programme.title: Ganges
programme.desc: The final leg of the river's journey sees it join its tributary the Brahmaputra. As the waters merge, they overspill their banks to fan out into hundreds of smaller rivulets and create the world's largest delta. The programme reveals how people and wildlife survive the inhospitable habitat, perhaps the last place on Earth where the Bengal tiger is still king

programme.title: Ganges
programme.desc: Exploring the story of the river, following its course from the Himalayas to their foothills. The programme reveals its enormous influence on the day-to-day living of a continent, how it has shaped Indian culture and religion and why it attracts a vast array of wildlife including snow leopards, Asiatic brown bears and bearded vultures

programme.title: Meat Loaf: In and Out of Hell
programme.desc: A profile of the rock star's life and career, reflecting on the music that propelled him to fame and charting his journey from an overweight, bullied child to his larger-than-life persona and icon status. Featuring an interview and performance by the singer, as well as backstage footage from a Las Vegas concert. The film also revisits the Dallas of Meat Loaf's early years and includes insights from high school friends who reveal where the unusual moniker really came from

programme.title: Sings the Beatles
programme.desc: A look back through the BBC's archives at memorable covers of classic Beatles songs. Included are a performance of Day Tripper by Sandie Shaw, Shirley Bassey's version of Something, the Carpenters' rendition of Help!, Oasis's reinvention of I Am the Walrus, and Joe Cocker's chart-topping interpretation of With a Little Help from My Friends. Plus, Paul McCartney's solo performance of Lady Madonna

programme.title: Secrets of Britain
programme.desc: The documentary series exploring the less well known side of Britain continues, with this edition exploring MI6, asking whether the shadowy world of espionage and counter-intelligence operations bear any resemblance to the James Bond novels

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth's Secret Agents
programme.desc: Documentary featuring dramatic reconstructions and interviews with historians to examine the network of spymasters and secret agents that helped protect Elizabeth I from assassination, terror and treason for more than 40 years. The first edition reveals how the pope's declaration of Elizabeth as a heretic 12 years into her reign set England's Catholics against her, leading the queen to charge William Cecil with the task of stopping assassins getting through, and the huge espionage network he established saw his spies break Catholic conspiracies at home and abroad
